complaint_what_happened:
I am a veteran and homeowner with a mortgage serviced by Freedom Mortgage. My issue involves improper handling of my escrow account. On XX/XX/2025, I discovered that my monthly mortgage payment had increased dramatically, from {$2600.00} to {$3600.00}, effective XX/XX/2025. The document titled XXXX Payment reflects this increased amount for the XXXX payment year. 

Upon review, I found that Freedom Mortgage had incorrectly added forced-placed home insurance to my escrow for the XXXX period, resulting in a significant and unjustified shortage. The document " XXXX Payment '' demonstrates : The correct XXXX monthly mortgage amount of {$2600.00}. 

An erroneous forced insurance charge of {$3800.00}. 

I contacted Freedom Mortgage on XX/XX/2025 to dispute both the escrow shortage and the forced insurance charge. I submitted valid proof of coverage through XXXX, including : Homeowners Insurance XXXX showing an active policy for {$1300.00}. 

XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXent confirming the XXXX premium had been paid in full and carried a {$0.00} balance. 

Escrow Payment and Analysis, which lists the correct XXXX premium, yet still includes the erroneous {$3800.00} forced insurance and reflects the inflated monthly payment. 

These documents confirm that : Freedom Mortgage was fully aware of my existing and valid insurance policy. 

The forced insurance was added in error and without cause. 

My escrow analysis and subsequent payment increase are based on false data. 

Despite providing this documentation, I have not received a satisfactory explanation or any written acknowledgment from Freedom Mortgage. The issue appears to have been dismissed without proper escalation or resolution. 

This error has caused a significant financial burden, increasing my monthly mortgage by nearly {$1000.00}, which is unsustainableespecially as a veteran on a fixed income. Moreover, this issue occurred while I was deployed overseas, which adds to the gravity and inappropriateness of the company 's actions. This experience has deeply eroded my trust in Freedom Mortgage. For a company that brands itself around the concept of freedom, it is disheartening to be treated in this wayparticularly while serving my country abroad. 

I respectfully demand the following corrective actions : Full reimbursement of the erroneous forced insurance charge of {$3800.00}. 

Recalculation of my escrow shortage, excluding the invalid insurance amount. 

Recalculation of my monthly escrow payments based on corrected figures. 

Adjustment of my total monthly mortgage payment to reflect accurate amounts. 

A written apology from Freedom Mortgage, mailed to an address of my choosing and emailed. 

A clear plan of action with a timeline from Freedom Mortgage explaining how they intend to resolve this issue.

I relied on this instruction in good faith. At no time did the servicer provide written notice that my account would be reported as delinquent during the review period, which is required before furnishing negative information to the credit bureaus. 

In response to my prior complaint, the servicer changed my credit reporting from XXXX  days past due to XXXX and XXXX days past due ( XX/XX/year> and XX/XX/year> ). While this partial correction acknowledges that the original reporting was inaccurate, the remaining XXXX delinquency is still incorrect and misleading. I was told no payments were required during the review period, and therefore any delinquency reporting for that timeframe is factually inaccurate.

Re : Complaint Against Select XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  for Bad Faith Servicing Practices, Failure to Disburse Escrow Payments, Retaliatory Conduct, and Disregard for Federal Consumer Protection Laws Dear Sir or Madam, I write to formally lodge a complaint against Select Portfolio Servicing ( SPS ) regarding its ongoing failure to administer escrow payments in accordance with its contractual and fiduciary obligations, its pattern of retaliatory conduct in response to my prior complaints filed with the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ), and its willful disregard for federal consumer protection laws. SPSs actions and omissions have caused me financial harm and undue distress, and I seek immediate corrective action. 

1. Failure to Timely Remit Property Tax Payments Pursuant to the terms of my loan agreement, SPS is responsible for the timely disbursement of escrow funds to pay my property taxes. Despite my multiple written and verbal notifications reminding SPS that my first-quarter property tax payment was due on XX/XX/year>XXXX SPS failed to remit payment as required. When confronted, SPS initially attributed the delinquency to a purported " special assessment. '' However, SPS later contradicted itself, falsely asserting that the payment was mailed on timean assertion that is demonstrably untrue. As a direct result of SPSs negligence, I incurred a late fee, which remains outstanding despite my repeated demands for reimbursement. 

2. Failure to Maintain Homeowners Insurance and Wrongful Policy Removal SPS has also failed to fulfill its duty to ensure the continuity of my homeowners insurance coverage. Despite my numerous faxes, phone calls, and written communications providing evidence of my valid insurance policy, SPS unjustifiably removed my coverage, citing an alleged " address discrepancy. '' This purported discrepancy is wholly pretextual and without merit, as my property at XXXX XXXX XXXX has always been designated in this manner, encompassing XXXX, XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX SPS has never previously raised any issue with this designation, and its sudden decision to reject my coverage under this pretense constitutes arbitrary and capricious conduct. 

3. Pattern of Retaliatory Conduct SPSs conduct is not merely a series of isolated servicing errors but rather appears to be a concerted effort to retaliate against me for exercising my rights under federal consumer protection laws. In addition to its failure to make timely escrow disbursements, SPS has demonstrated a deliberate unwillingness to communicate in good faith or provide a reasonable resolution to these matters. This pattern of conduct constitutes bad faith servicing and may violate applicable federal regulations governing mortgage servicers, including the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act ( RESPA ) and the Consumer Financial Protection Act ( CFPA ).

4. SPSs Assertion That CFPB Lacks Authority Most troublingly, SPS has outright stated that the CFPB has no authority to regulate its actions, that RESPA does not apply to its servicing obligations, and that I, as a borrower, have no rights or protections under federal law. This assertion is not only legally erroneous but reflects an alarming disregard for established consumer protection statutes and regulatory oversight. If SPS believes itself to be beyond the reach of federal consumer protection laws, this raises serious concerns about its broader servicing practices and its treatment of borrowers nationwide.

Formal Complaint Against LoanCare for Wrongful Credit Reporting During Approved Forbearance/Deferment To Whom It May Concern, I am submitting this complaint against LoanCare , LLC regarding the wrongful and damaging reporting of 90-day late payments to all three credit reporting agencies ( XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX  ), despite the fact that I was not late and was fully placed under an approved forbearance/deferment plan. 
On XX/XX/year>, LoanCare issued me an official Forbearance Plan Offer Reduced Payment, showing that I was approved to make {$0.00} payments beginning XX/XX/year> through XX/XX/year>. ( Document attached. ) This means I was not required to make any payments during that period and therefore could not legally be reported late. 
However, despite being under a valid forbearance, LoanCare reported me as 30, 60, and 90 days late, which is a clear violation of federal credit reporting laws.

________________________________________ CREDIT LAWS VIOLATED 1. CARES Act Section 4021 If a consumers loan is placed in an accommodation ( such as forbearance, deferment, or payment relief ), and the account was current before the accommodation, the furnisher must continue to report the account as current during the accommodation period.

LoanCare violated this law by reporting me late while I was under an officially approved forbearance plan.

2. Fair Credit Reporting Act ( FCRA ) 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( a ) Furnishers are legally required to report information that is accurate and complete.

Reporting late payments while a borrower is in a deferment/forbearance program is inaccurate and causes serious and unlawful harm.

3. FCRA Duty to Correct and Update Information ( 15 U.S.C. 1681s-2 ( a ) ( 2 ) ) If a furnisher discovers that the information furnished is incorrect, they must promptly update and correct it.

LoanCare has failed to correct the false late-payment reporting.

________________________________________ Nature of the Harm Because LoanCare wrongfully reported 90-day late payments, I have suffered : Significant damage to my credit score Denial of credit opportunities Emotional and financial distress Difficulty qualifying for housing and financial services These negative marks are entirely false and occurred only because LoanCare failed to follow federal law regarding forbearance reporting.

________________________________________ Requested Resolution I respectfully request the CFPB to : 1. Investigate LoanCare for unlawful credit reporting practices.

2. Require LoanCare to immediately remove all late-payment reporting from my credit files during the deferment/forbearance period.

3. Require LoanCare to provide written confirmation that the corrections have been made with all three credit bureaus.

4. Review whether LoanCare is systematically misreporting consumers in forbearance.

________________________________________ Supporting Documentation Attached LoanCare Forbearance Plan Letter ( dated XX/XX/year> ) Copies of credit reports showing XXXX late reporting ________________________________________ Thank you for your time and attention to this matter. I request urgent action, as these wrongful late marks continue to harm me.

sub_issue: Changes in loan terms during the application process
complaint_what_happened:
I am submitting this complaint regarding a Home Equity Line of Credit ( HELOC ) application I submitted with Bank of America. Before the loan was closed, Bank of America submitted a request to my homeowners insurance provider to modify my existing policy. This change was made without my explicit consent or knowledge. 

When I inquired, the loan officer stated that the request was based on a generic Authorization to Release Information clause I had signed during the application. However, the language of that form only authorized verification of my financial and employment information. It did not authorize executing policy changes or altering third-party contracts. 

This action resulted in changes to my insurance policy that I did not authorize and were made before the loan was closed. I only became aware of the change after the fact. I believe this represents an inappropriate or premature action taken by the lender, and I am requesting the CFPB investigate whether Bank of America 's conduct violated lending standards or consumer rights. 

I am also concerned that the unauthorized policy modification could interfere with escrow payment routing or trigger a lapse in coverage if not corrected promptly. I have reached out to the insurance company, but they have not yet responded. This creates an ongoing risk that I am actively trying to prevent, solely due to the bank 's actions prior to loan closing. 

Bank of America has cited the attached Authorization to Release Information as the basis for their request to modify my homeowners insurance policy before loan closing. However, after reviewing the language, it is clear that the document only authorizes verification of financial, employment, and credit information. 

It does not contain any language explicitly authorizing the bank to initiate changes to third-party insurance policies, submit policyholder modifications, or direct insurance billing changes on my behalf. 

Therefore, I am submitting this document for reference as part of my complaint, to demonstrate that the action taken by the bank exceeded the scope of what was authorized.

complaint_what_happened:
I am a homeowner with a mortgage serviced by PHH Mortgage. I have been actively working with PHH on a loan modification / loss mitigation application since XX/XX/XXXX. My most recent packet was due on XX/XX/XXXX, and I submitted the complete packet early, around XX/XX/XXXX. I even recorded myself preparing and sending the packet to document that I included all required documents. 
However, I then received a new PHH packet dated XX/XX/XXXX and postmarked XX/XX/XXXX, stating that my prior loan modification was denied and that I needed to submit a new packet. This means PHH denied my application before they could have reasonably reviewed the documents that were due on XX/XX/XXXX and submitted around XX/XX/XXXX. This package was also due XX/XX/XXXX. However, I did not receive it in my mailbox until XX/XX/XXXX which is past the date of submission. 
I have repeatedly requested that PHH provide a written denial letter explaining the specific reason ( s ) for denying my complete loan modification application, but PHH has not provided any written explanation. All explanations have been verbal over the phone. 
My understanding under RESPA Regulation X ( 12 C.F.R. 1024.41 ) is that when a servicer denies a complete loss mitigation application for a loan modification, the servicer must send a written notice stating the specific reason ( s ) for the denial. PHH has not done so. 
I have also been dealing with a complicated situation because the original borrower ( my mother ) passed away unexpectedly. I inherited the home due to her unexpected death. I did not fall behind on a mortgage for a house I irresponsibly took on ; instead, I am trying to manage a loan that was in my mothers name when she died. I was told that, given the borrower is deceased, the amount owed should have been placed on hold and that I could not be personally liable for that amount in the same way until my status was resolved. 
When I started this process, the amount past due was roughly {$4000.00} {$6000.00}, and I was making payments to reduce that balance. PHH then instructed me to begin the loan modification process because the borrower ( my mother ) is deceased. Despite this, the amount they say is owed has continued to increase dramatically with each denied modification. 
I have continued to make monthly payments, but PHH has indicated that these payments under the attempted modification do not count toward the increasing past-due balance tied to the previous borrower. As a result, that balance has grown and is now in excess of {$20000.00}, which is not an amount I can realistically afford. It feels as though every time I try to comply and send what is requested, the balance gets larger and the modification is denied without a clear written explanation. 
Another complication arose because my mothers mortgage payments were being made directly from her bank account. After her death, that account was closed. PHH would not allow me to simply change the bank information or make smaller payments from a different account ; I was told I had to pay the full amount due in one lump sum from a separate account. This made it extremely difficult to stay current and contributed to the hardship that led me into the loss mitigation process in the first place. 
In my interactions with PHH, I have also experienced poor treatment on the phone, including representatives talking over me, making comments such as speak up maam, and refusing to connect me with a supervisor when I asked. This has made an already stressful situation even more overwhelming. It feels predatory and like an intentional infliction of stress rather than an attempt to help a struggling successor-in-interest keep a home. 
I am facing serious hardship and am trying in good faith to keep my home. This home is the last piece of my mother that I have. She was a public servant and teacher for over 30 years and worked hard for this house. I am doing everything I can to preserve it. I have kept copies of my completed packet, the PHH denial packet and envelope ( showing the dates ), and notes of multiple phone calls with PHH representatives.

I believe PHH has : Denied my loss mitigation request prematurely Failed to provide the required written denial with specific reasons Failed to respond in writing to my request for the reason
( s ) for denial Allowed the past-due amount to grow to an unaffordable level while I am actively making payments and seeking assistance I am asking for help because I am trying to act in good faith, follow PHHs instructions, and save my home after an unexpected death in my family, but PHHs handling of my loan and loss mitigation applications has made that extremely difficult.

complaint_what_happened:
During 2023, my spouse and I contacted our mortgage servicer, M & T Bank, to request assistance through a loan modification for our FHA mortgage. While the loan modification was under review, we were informed that mortgage payments were not required during the modification review period. 

Based on this guidance, we acted in good faith and did not submit payments while the modification review was pending. Despite this, the servicer later reported multiple late payments to the credit bureaus for the same months that the loan modification was under active review. 

The late payments reported appear as a single consecutive group during 2023. These late payments were not the result of neglect or unwillingness to pay, but rather occurred because we relied on the servicers instructions during the modification process. 

Once the loan modification review concluded and we were informed that payments were required, we resumed making payments immediately. The account has remained current since that time and is presently in good standing. 

We later contacted the servicer to request correction or removal of the late payment reporting, explaining that the non-payments occurred based on the servicers guidance during the loan modification review. That request was denied without resolving the underlying issue. 

The continued reporting of these late payments does not accurately reflect our payment obligation during the loan modification review period and is causing significant and ongoing harm to our credit profile. 

I am requesting that this matter be reviewed by M & T Banks XXXX department and that the late payment reporting associated with the loan modification review period be corrected or removed, or otherwise updated to accurately reflect that payments were not required during that time.

Instead of accommodating this, they have XXXX my access to their online servicing platform, further impeding my ability to manage my loan.

I am asking the CFPB to investigate Evergreen Home Loans for potential loan servicing violations, discriminatory or retaliatory communication restrictions, and harmful mismanagement of borrower records.

issue: Applying for a mortgage or refinancing an existing mortgage

complaint_what_happened:
In XX/XX/XXXX, my husband and I decided to refinance our home, which was almost paid off, to a VA XXXX refinance loan. This would allow us to pay down our existing high-interest-rate debt, remove my mother-in-law from the loan and deed, lower our interest rate, and undertake some home renovations. I did a general inquiry online, and XXXX contacted me. I immediately trusted NewDay USA because they advertised that the XXXX supported them and took pride in XXXX. XXXX XXXX made promises he did not keep on behalf of NewDay USA. He promised the process would be quick, pledged to pay off all my debt, and give me cash to renovate my home at the full amount my house was worth. I was quoted at 7.8 %. 
I trusted XXXX because of how they represented the military and its XXXX. At some point during the refinance process, my interest rate increased without my knowledge. I was not offered a 100 % cash-out refinance, my mother-in-law was not removed from the deed, and I was inundated with excessive loan documents. I received a new packet via XXXX every few days from XXXX. I realize now they were trying to confuse me to take advantage of me. I am still being misled to this day. I had a sewage backup in my home back in XXXX. I was in a loan modification because I had missed some payments due to a family emergency that set me back. 
I called XXXX and asked them for a forbearance, and they told me this was not an option, when in fact, I believe it was an option. My mother-in-law lived XXXX miles away from us in XXXX direction. So about an XXXX  round trip, and was bedridden. My husband and I had to care for her. Often, we had XXXX XXXX over more than once a day to ensure she was cared for. My husband lost time at work, and the snowball effect on our finances has been significant. XXXX offered me a loan modification that would lower my interest rate, but nothing else would change. On XX/XX/XXXX, I went into a loan modification. My loan modification process began with our first set of documents, which required my mother-in-law 's signature. She was supposed to be removed from the deed. I contacted XXXX, and they needed me to send a death certificate, but they would never answer my question as to whether she was on the deed. I know she was because why would she need to sign the new loan modification documents? During the loan modification process, I was not required to make any payments on my house until the modification was complete. Yet, I had to send {$3000.00} in money orders, which I do not see posted to my account to date. Not to mention, they were reporting me delinquent to the credit bureaus and wreaking havoc on my credit. The next set of documents for loan modification I received was supposed to have my mother-in-law 's name removed. I signed and sent them back. Then, XXXX contacts me and says they made a clerical error and will be changing a disclosure on the modification. That still did not matter because I received another call and another set of documents with another change on them. They had to put my husband as the one who was not responsible for the loan if I became deceased. I am not sure how accurate any of these corrections were, but I began to see a pattern of inundating me with paperwork, and I became very suspicious. I began researching my bills and started asking questions. That is when I noticed my interest rate was 7.9 %. I was confused. Not what I was quoted at, nor promised. I saw " corporate advances '' on my bill. I called and asked what these were for. The first guy was a customer service representative. He had no clue, but came back and told me they were legal fees from when the company had to drive by my house to verify I still lived here. What? I was in a loan modification. Why would someone need to drive by to confirm if I still lived here? He was then eager to rush me over to the loan modification because he could not answer my questions. He sent me to the loan modification department, where I spoke to a supervisor who also could not answer any of my questions. He had submitted a request for a supervisor to contact me and answer my questions. It took them several days to contact me, and I still have no answers to any of my questions. He was supposed to call me back, but I have not heard a peep from anyone. Not only did I have " corporate advance '' charges that no one could verify, but I also had a " suspense account '' with funds in it. They told me that when I paid extra, the money went into a " suspense account '' until I had a full payment to make a regular payment with. The other representative told me it only stayed in there for XXXX  days before being applied to the principal. 
However, a representative on XXXX of my calls to make a payment stated that any overage on my payment would be immediately applied to my principal. XXXX different versions. Who do I believe? None of them. Suppose you recall the sewage backup in my home during the loan modification, which heightened my suspicions and prompted me to investigate the handling of my mortgage by NewDay USA. Because I was in a loan modification and had more than {$36000.00} in damages to my home, when my insurance company cut the check, it had both my name and XXXX 's name on it, as it was for {$27000.00}. They have a vested interest in my property. When I filed a property claim on their website, they informed me that they would put my money in a restricted escrow account and divide it into XXXX installments after I provided them with quotes, a signed contract they offered, and other required documents. 
I was furious. I should not have still been in loan modification ; they could not get the paperwork correct. Again, not my fault. However, I was not given any other option. They would not provide my XXXX with his 30 % upfront fee after I made a written request to them, along with proof from my XXXX. They sent me 25 %. 
Additionally, I had to pay for overnight shipping labels or wait XXXX to XXXX business days for each check to be processed, and another XXXX to XXXX  days in the mail if I did not provide the labels. I was told I could only use XXXX at this time. I had to wait XXXX to XXXX  days for inspection. XXXX to XXXX  days for inspection results to be uploaded and approved, XXXX to XXXX  days for the check to be processed and mailed, XXXX to XXXX  days for the check to be sent to me, and XXXX  days for my check to be processed and funds released by the bank. I have lived in a hotel for XXXX of the XXXX months thus far, and the remaining XXXX months, I have lived in my home without a kitchen. I am not sure if you have ever tried to remodel a home with less than all the money to buy the necessary supplies. I have missed sales, and eating out is costing me a pretty XXXX. My XXXX quit because he was losing jobs due to the process taking too long to secure funds, and the lack of ability to obtain all the necessary materials, as well as manage the project effectively. That left my husband and me to get the job done, which meant my husband lost months at work, making sure his new business became profitable. I tried reasoning with XXXX, begging, anger, and compromise, among other approaches. They did not care how much money this was costing my husband and me. To add insult to injury, when we reached the 75 % mark, we did our inspection, and they did not send the final 25 %. So, I called them, and they told me I needed a score of 76 % to receive the remaining funds. So, my husband and I worked into the night to reach the 76 % mark, rushed the inspection with the inspection company, and XXXX sent approximately {$270.00} ( I do not recall the exact amount ). I call them again. I am on the phone with them several times throughout the day, and XXXX call lasted XXXX hours, most of which I spent on hold. I was informed that I needed to reach 100 % to receive the remaining funds. Different than what I was told before, but I was furious. How do I complete a job to 100 % without having 100 % of the resources? I spoke to people from all departments, who have recorded conversations, stating that I needed to provide an estimate of the cost to replace my kitchen, and it would be submitted for review and approval or denial. They have delayed construction for XXXX weeks this time, and I still do not have the funds, but they are on their way. None of this makes any sense to me. That money was for me to renovate my home. I ended up having another snowball effect, and bills were late, and I was paying late fees and living without a kitchen and an XXXX  child. My husband lost time at work to ensure his new business was a success. He could not afford to buy a product that would cost him customers, and his assets were lost because of what XXXX had done to me and my family. 
I still have no resolution on the mortgage statements. They refused to give them to me. I forgot to mention, I was no longer in a XXXX fixed-rate mortgage. Remember, I was only supposed to have my interest rate adjusted. No, they decided without my approval to put me in a XXXX capitalized something or other. I still have no idea what they are doing with my mortgage, but what I am sure of is that they are not playing fair, overcharging me, charging me late fees when I am not late ( per a supervisor I spoke to ), and put me further in debt with the handling of my loss to my home and delaying construction, which I am certain is illegal or frowned upon in the state of XXXX. I also forgot to mention that someone forged my name on XXXX of the documents I signed at closing with a notary. I received an email from XXXX stating my signature looked different from the rest on XXXX of the closing documents. I have the email, and I will share it with you. However, should I not have signed that document, someone forged my signature with a notary, as I did with the other closing documents? I feel betrayed, angry, and disgruntled by how XXXX has treated me. They have put me further into debt with their misleading tactics and false representations. How can someone who represents the XXXX  and its XXXX mislead us into thinking we are being told the truth and provide us with all our options instead of options that make them money? It is shameful, and my debt is worse than when I switched over to them. I have more documents to submit, but it will not allow me to upload anymore plus I have phone recordings as well.

sub_issue: Applying for or obtaining a modification, forbearance plan, short sale, or deed-in-lieu
complaint_what_happened:
To Whom It May Concern, I am submitting this complaint against my mortgage servicer, Select Portfolio Servicing ( SPS ), regarding their continued failure to process my loan modification request, despite my repeated efforts and full cooperation over the past XXXX months. 

I initially reached out to SPS in XX/XX/XXXX seeking a loan modification due to a sudden and complete loss of income following the downturn in the housing and mortgage market. I have been in the mortgage industry for over XXXX years as a loan originator and processor. When the market collapsed at the end of XXXX, it wiped out my income, depleted my savings, and left me without any resources to maintain my regular mortgage payments. 

Despite this, SPS informed me that because I was not yet delinquent, they would not review me for assistance. In good faith, I attempted to stay current while also requesting help. I had no income in XXXX, XXXX, or XXXX, and by the end of XXXX, I finally secured employment. However, my income was not enough to cover the mortgage and my living expenses as a single mother of XXXX, who also at XXXX point had to pay child support. 

Even while I was unemployed or underemployed, I submitted the requested income documentation repeatedly including paystubs, hardship letters, and bank statements throughout XXXX, XXXX, and XXXX. During that time, I received little to no communication from SPS, nor any indication that my request was being reviewed in good faith. Instead, I was met with repeated requests for the same documents, many of which I had already submitted, causing unnecessary delays and emotional and financial stress. 

Despite the lack of assistance, I managed by borrowing and scraping together funds to catch up and pay XXXX through XXXX mortgage payments on my own. In XX/XX/XXXX, I had to leave my job they were not honest organization stealing from people in contracting business,, though I am still actively working to earn and maintain stability as mortgage loan officer. However, the monthly mortgage payment of over {$4000.00} is still unaffordable based on my current income. I am requesting a modification with a lower monthly payment or for the arrears to be capitalized, so I can regain stability and avoid falling behind again. 

At this point, I feel that SPS is not handling my request in a fair, transparent, or timely manner. As a mortgage professional myself, I am fully aware of the procedures and know that loan modifications are a viable option in hardship situations especially when the borrower is willing and cooperative, as I have been throughout this process. 

I am asking the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau to investigate this matter and require SPS to : Review my hardship and modification request promptly and in good faith Stop the repeated and unnecessary requests for documents already provided Offer a realistic payment solution, such as a rate reduction, term extension, or principal forbearance Provide clear communication and transparency regarding next steps My goal is not to avoid payment, but to get back on solid footing with a payment I can actually sustain long-term. Without intervention, I risk slipping back into default not for lack of effort, but because my servicer refuses to work with me. 

Thank you for your time and attention to this urgent matter. 

Sincerely,

complaint_what_happened: I purchased a new home with XXXX XXXX and financing had to be with NVR Inc, the builders Lender. The Loan Officer assigned was not knowledgeable and did not disclose important information about the lock/loan process. When it came to locking the loan, I was told I would be able to get a float down in the rate option, assuming if the rates were lower than lock rate, I proceeded with locking at a 5.99 % rate. When it came time for me to exercise this float down option, I was told that the lock length period that was chosen prevented me from taking advantage of the lower rate. I pointed out to them that I was not aware of this, was not disclosed this information by the Loan Officer and in fact other builders in the area were advertising 4.875 % rates. Subsequently I purchased a smaller unit in a different lot and block. The Loan Officer did not know how to transfer the loan information to the new property and was told he would check with management. This took a couple of weeks from when I resigned the new contract to obtain new loan documents. At this time, I was told that I had to keep the same rate of 5.99 % which was at a substantial cost, and took up quite a bit of my closing cost incentive offered by the builder. Once I received the Loan documents they appeared to look the same as the initial ones I received, so I proceeded in signing. My home is now about XXXX days from being completed, and was told that I was cleared to close. I informed the Loan processor that I had to help my grandson get a new vehicle due to his truck becoming undriveable. I co-signed for him, but knew that this was not an issue with me still qualifying based on my income and having very minimal debt obligation. The Manger at NVR called me and told me she had good and bad news. Good news was, I still qualified but not under the program they placed me under because that program had income limitations which supposedly helped keep my rate at 5.99 %. Apparently, backed out my Social security income I receive to keep me incme low and now that I cosigned for my grandson, they have to add back in the income that they knew about from the beginning of my loan application. But at no point in time was I told about this program and what it entailed. Now they want to give me a higher rate or pay {$1100.00} more in closing cost to keep the 5.99 %. It is absolutely ridiculous getting a {$230000.00} home that my closing cost would be almost {$18000.00}. I feel like this was a bait and switch program to cover for the initial issue with the float down of the lock issue. I understand that obtaining new credit during a loan application is not something that should be done, but if you do not know about what they were doing behind the scene to cover up mistakes, how can you know how to proceed correctly NVR should be held accountable for mistakes and do right by the consumers. Thank you for your assistance and hearing my issues. XXXX

Here 's how their ties work, particularly in relation to purchasing wrongful foreclosure homes from veterans : * Property Management Services : The VA contracts with XXXX to manage these acquired REO properties. This includes a wide range of services such as eviction, property preservation, repairs, tax/HOA management, title work, marketing, offer management, contracts, closing, and invoicing.

XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX : The entire process of managing a VA REO property is housed within XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX This system stores information about former owners ( the borrowers associated with the defaulted loan ), property occupants, and potential/actual purchasers.

* Information Exchange : There's a continuous exchange of information between the VA 's systems ( like XXXX and XXXX ) and XXXX  The VA sends information about properties and former owners to XXXX XXXX XXXX  sends back details regarding title, listings, offers, and invoices. 
* VA Vendee Loan Program : XXXX XXXX XXXX ( an affiliate of XXXX  ) offers a specific financing program called the VA Vendee Loan Program. This program allows qualified borrowers ( including veterans and non-veterans ) to purchase VA-owned REO properties with competitive rates and often little to no money down. This essentially facilitates the sale of properties that the VA has acquired through foreclosure.

Regarding " wrongful foreclosure homes from Veterans '' : It's important to clarify that XXXX XXXX role is primarily in managing and selling properties that have already been foreclosed upon and acquired by the VA. The VA, through its loan guarantee program, aims to help veterans avoid foreclosure in the first place. 
If our foreclosure is deemed wrongful, it would typically involve issues with the loan servicer 's actions before the foreclosure process was completed and the property becomes VA-owned REO. The VA has a strong commitment to helping veterans avoid foreclosure and offers various assistance programs and resources, including : * Foreclosure Avoidance : The VA encourages veterans facing mortgage difficulties to contact their loan servicers immediately and also offers its own VA loan technicians to provide personalized support and explore options like repayment plans, special forbearances, and loan modifications.

* Veterans Affairs Servicing Purchase ( VASP ) Program : This program ( though recently undergoing changes ) allowed the VA to purchase delinquent loans from servicers, becoming the primary loan servicer to provide veterans with a stable payment plan and prevent foreclosure.

* Legal Action : There have been instances where veterans have filed lawsuits regarding wrongful foreclosures, particularly after certain VA forbearance programs ended.We are seeking a wrongful Forclosure do to strong evidence that has been presented in the fullness of the life of the Loan While XXXX  plays a crucial role in the post-foreclosure process for VA-acquired properties, their direct involvement isn't in the prevention or resolution of " wrongful foreclosures '' themselves. That responsibility largely falls on the VA 's loan guaranty program, the loan servicers, and potentially legal avenues for veterans.We have already reached out to the VA OIG and we have been told that the entire project will be thoroughly Based on the evidence presentation this is a colossal issue that may impact the whole VA NOTICE WRITTEN Process

sub_issue: Trying to communicate with the company to fix an issue while managing or servicing your loan
complaint_what_happened:
I am submitting this complaint regarding ongoing servicing misconduct and lack of authority by my mortgage servicer. 

On or about XX/XX/year>, I sent a written demand requesting proof of ownership and authority to enforce the promissory note and deed of trust, including the original promissory note, complete chain of endorsements, any allonge, proof of possession, and transfer or assignment documentation. 

On XX/XX/year>, the servicer responded. However, the documents provided were incomplete and defective. The response did not include a complete chain of endorsements from the original lender, did not demonstrate lawful transfer of the note, did not provide proof of possession of the original promissory note, and did not include valid bills of sale or transfer agreements establishing standing to collect or foreclose. 

Despite these deficiencies, the servicer has continued collection and servicing activity and recently attempted telephone contact through its loss mitigation department. This occurred while the debt and standing remain formally disputed and unresolved. 

On XX/XX/year>, I sent a written cease-and-desist and challenge to standing directing that all communications be in writing only and reiterating that the servicer lacks authority to enforce the loan based on its own documentation. 

The servicers failure to provide competent proof of standing, combined with continued collection and loss mitigation contact, constitutes servicing misconduct, misrepresentation of authority, and improper handling of a disputed mortgage loan. 

I am requesting that the CFPB review this matter, require the servicer to cease unlawful collection activity, and compel corrective action consistent with federal servicing requirements.

I bought a house with my partner in XX/XX/XXXX as " tenancy in common. '' We separated in XX/XX/XXXX. He moved out at that time, and I took over sole financial responsibility of the mortgage payments. This can be verified with my bank statements as payments came directly from my personal checking account. After 5 months of trying to sell the house, I decided to keep it and refinance to get his name off. RocketMortgage told me it would take a little over 2 weeks and that I would not have to pay my XXXX or XXXX mortgage payments. However, RocketMortgage took 2 months ( beginning at the end of XXXX and ending in beginning of XXXX ) to complete the refinance process and told me to go ahead and pay the XXXX mortgage payment since the refinancing process may not be complete in time to prevent the late payment hitting my credit. I was told that this mortgage payment would either be rolled into my new mortgage or would be refunded to me. I had not heard from anyone about the refund, so called to verify the status. It was at that time that I was informed that a check had already been sent out to an address that I was not familiar with. I learned that it was my ex 's new address. Rocketmortgage did not attempt to contact me to verify the correct mailing address, but even so, I would presume they would know to mail it to me as I have been the one in contact with them and was refinancing the same property with them. The rep said she could re-route the check to my property address, but that it would still have both of our names on it and there would be no way to circumvent needing both signatures to cash the check. I called again about a week later because I still had not received the check in the mail. I was then notified that my ex had called to have the check cancelled and instead have the payment direct deposited into his personal account. When I confronted RocketMortgage about this error, they said there was nothing that they could do since his name was on the old loan. Rocketmortgage must have been the ones to call and notify him of the refund, because he otherwise would not have been aware yet. Although I am a joint tenant on the loan, they never made an attempt to contact me to confirm my verbal approval of this change to deposit it into his account. My name is also not on the account to which it was deposited, as we never had any joint accounts. I do not understand why both of our signatures would have been required to deposit the check, but my signature or verbal approval was not needed for him to route the money to his personal account. I also do not understand why this direct deposit option was not provided to me when I mentioned to the rep that it would be difficult to get his signature on the check as we had not been in contact. This matter was brought to RocketMortgage 's executive leadership, but they made no attempt to resolve this issue. They washed their hands of any responsibility for the error even after I pointed out the injustice. They said that they did not check if the receiving bank account had both names on it because it was less than their threshold of {$4500.00}. The refund was {$4400.00}. It apparently does not matter that we were joint owners ; they only pay attention to the first name listed on the account, and the second person listed on the loan has no say and does not even get contacted about any changes.

I am filing a complaint against Shellpoint Mortgage Servicing regarding improper lossmitigation handling, dual tracking, and unreasonable demands during my request for a deed in lieu of foreclosure. 

I am the former borrower on a mortgage for the property at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, Washington, DC XXXX. My personal liability on this mortgage was discharged in Chapter 7 bankruptcy on XX/XX/year>. I do not want to keep the property and I submitted a complete loss mitigation package to Shellpoint requesting a deed in lieu of foreclosure. 

Despite submitting all documents requested, Shellpoint continues to move forward with judicial foreclosure at the same time they are processing my deed in lieu request. This appears to be a violation of the federal servicing rules under Regulation X, which prohibit a servicer from moving forward with foreclosure while a complete loss mitigation application is pending.

Shellpoint has now informed me that they need a free and clear deed in order to move forward with the deed in lieu. This requirement is impossible for me to meet because I am not the current titleholder of the property under normal circumstances until the deed in lieu is executed. A deed in lieu is the mechanism that returns title to the lender. I can not produce a free and clear deed before loss mitigation is completed, and Shellpoint knows this. This appears to be an unreasonable and abusive servicing requirement.

Shellpoint is effectively forcing the foreclosure to continue by imposing a condition that a borrower can not meet. This prevents my deed in lieu request from being processed, while foreclosure activity continues in violation of the nodualtracking rule ( 12 C.F.R. 1024.41 ( g ) ).

I want to make clear that I do not want the property and am trying to surrender it. I submitted the requested forms, including Form 710 and additional documentation. Shellpoint has told me the file is incomplete unless I sign a Loss Mitigation OptOut Form, even though I have already requested a deed in lieu. The optout form is typically used to decline loss mitigation, but they are requiring it as a condition of reviewing a deed in lieu. This is confusing, misleading, and appears designed to both move foreclosure forward and avoid offering the deed in lieu option that I am requesting.

I am asking the CFPB to require Shellpoint to : 1. Stop all foreclosure activity immediately while my deed in lieu request is reviewed.

2. Process my deed in lieu without requiring an impossible free and clear deed from me.

3. Confirm in writing whether they will approve or deny the deed in lieu.

4. Stop using forms or requirements that appear to conflict with bankruptcy discharge protections and federal servicing rules.

I am providing copies of communications, my complete loss mitigation application, bankruptcy discharge, and Shellpoints statements regarding the need for a free and clear deed.

Thank you

I have also tried contacting UWM by email using the addresses listed on their website and customer portal, but they do not work. The following email addresses were used : XXXX the message was returned as undeliverable ( Address not found ). 

XXXX the email was sent successfully, but I never received a response. 
These are the same contact emails provided by UWM for borrowers who need assistance, and neither of them has been helpful. This shows that UWM is making it extremely difficult for homeowners like me to communicate or resolve urgent issues related to forbearance and foreclosure.

I am a single mother of XXXX dependents ( ages XXXX and XXXX ), and I am doing everything I can to keep my home. I respectfully request your help to resolve this matter quickly before any further damage occurs.

issue: Applying for a mortgage or refinancing an existing mortgage
sub_issue: Changes in loan terms during the application process
complaint_what_happened:
I was under contract for an investment property. I had a DSCR loan for 75 % of ARV ( After Repair Value ). The appraisal should have easily come back at XXXX but it came back at XXXX, because the appraiser did a current value appraisal instead of an ARV. We could not get appraiser to change it so I was forced to find another lender. I searched the web and found American Heritage Lending. I completed a form online under the " Investor '' tab for a " fix and flip. '' I spoke with a lender, and she said she could process my loan in the time I needed and she said they could do a desk appraisal and assured me it would be an ARV appraisal.

She told me she could give me 90 % loan to cost. That means she could give me 93 % of the total amount of the loan. She gave me a long list of items she needed and I had them returned to her within the hour. As an investor I have done this several times before. I also had two other lenders who could do the same thing, one with the same 93 %, but 4 went with them because of the desk appraisal, which meant they did not need to send anyone out to the house. She told me she would have my disclosures the next morning. 

She pulled my hard credit immediately, which was unusual. This was a DCSR loan. I knew she needed to verify me as a guarantor on my business loan, but generally they don't do a hard credit pull right away. Then one of the documents she asked for was a list of previous deals. Most lenders need 5, she asked for 6. I gave her 6, but the sixth one was not yet complete, so she said she could only give me 90 % of the full loan instead of 93 %. I wasn't happy because I could have gone with a different lender. I asked if I could appeal the decision. She said if I had more collateral, such as a 402K. So I sent her a 401K statement that could cover the full amount of the loan. I again asked for the disclosureI was supposed to get yesterday morning. She said she was waiting to hear back about the 93 %. She came back with the decision that they could only give me 90 %. I wasn't happy -- you, but I had just had my due diligence period extended and I was on a serious time crunch. So I let it go. 

After XXXX days of asking for the disclosures, she sent an estimate and I called right away because it had a down payment of 25 %. She said to disregard that. She said she hadn't even submitted my loan and didn't even know where I saw that. 

Two days later, she sends the disclosures. She has all of my current bills listed as if I was getting a personal loan. And even with that, the down payment was 26 % and closing costs were XXXX. I couldn't believe it! She explained that we discussed a rehab loan. There is mot even an option for a rehab loan under the investor tab. She tried to explain the numbers, but it was a futile effort. I believe I was set up. That the advertisement for 93 % ltc was just to get your application. They had no intention of giving me that loan. I have excellent credit and proof that I could cover the loan. I think they purposely slowly processed my loan, hoping I would have no choice but to go with them. This was a bait and switch deceptive practice. I could lose my deal because of this deception. I am concerned about all of the personal documents and information. I don't trust this company.

complaint_what_happened:
Subject : Formal Grievance Regarding Deceptive and Misrepresented Loan Terms XXXX XXXX for Member Number XXXX / Loan XXXX Dear Mosaic and XXXX XXXX XXXX, We are writing to formally raise a serious grievance regarding the solar panel financing arrangement established in connection with our installation facilitated through XXXX XXXX XXXX and his solar company, XXXX XXXX. While XXXX XXXX and his representatives were the point of contact during the purchase, the financing of the solar panel system was ultimately administered by your institutions, and therefore, the responsibility for the terms and their enforcement rests in large part with you. 
At the time of purchase and contract execution, we were clearly and repeatedly assured both verbally and in writing via email correspondence, proposals, and related materials that the loan we were entering into would be fully transferable to a future homeowner in the event of a sale. This representation was pivotal in our decision to move forward with the system and associated financing. 
Having owned solar in the past, we are acutely aware of how important loan transferability is to preserving both the value and marketability of a property. It is not only a matter of convenience but one of financial prudence. We entered into this agreement in good faith, relying on these documented assurances. 
Now, nearly four years later, as we prepare to sell our home, we have discovered to our shock and serious detriment that the loan is in fact non-transferable, and that any future buyer would be prohibited from assuming it. This revelation contradicts all prior representations made to us and has placed us in a highly vulnerable financial and legal position. 
Had we known this at the outset, we would have categorically declined the loan. The presence of this untransferable lien now significantly impairs our ability to sell our home, a condition we were explicitly promised would not occur. 
This constitutes : Misrepresentation and deceptive lending practices Breach of contract or implied good-faith agreement A potential violation of the Truth in Lending Act ( TILA ) An unfair and predatory lending arrangement, especially given that this feature was misrepresented at the time of loan origination We are not only disappointed we are now actively burdened by the consequences of a financial product that was sold to us under false pretenses. Regardless of who physically made the representations, Mosaic and XXXX XXXX XXXX, as the lending entities, are directly responsible for the administration, disclosure, and enforcement of the loan terms. It is your obligation to ensure your financial products are clear, fair, and legally compliant, and that any third-party agents or dealers representing them do so accurately. 
Accordingly, we hereby demand the following : Written confirmation that the loan will be made fully transferable, as was originally promised and presented to us at the time of agreement ; or The immediate removal of the solar panel system from our property and a full refund of all payments made to date, including interest, penalties, and any related costs incurred. 
Additionally, please see the attached invoice provided by XXXX, which reflects the terms discussed, including the assurance that the loan would be fully transferable, and shows that the system was paid for under those expectations.

sub_issue: Trying to communicate with the company to fix an issue while managing or servicing your loan
complaint_what_happened:
I am selling my home and trying to close on time. My mortgage servicer, New American Funding, is providing inconsistent information and poor communication, and I am unable to obtain the documentation and breakdown needed to understand and verify a large deferred balance and escrow overdraft included in my payoff. I am unable to reach or be routed to the correct department, and my requests for documentation are not being properly addressed. This is putting my pending closing at risk. 

I received a payoff statement dated XX/XX/XXXX showing a total payoff of {$280000.00}, including a Payment Deferred Balance of {$35000.00} and Escrow/Impound Overdraft of {$7300.00}, along with other fees and advances ( late charges, corporate advances, and outstanding fees ). This payoff amount makes it difficult to close and requires immediate clarification and documentation.

I have attempted multiple times by phone and email to : 1. obtain a clear explanation and calculation of the Payment Deferred Balance ( {$35000.00} ) 2. obtain escrow documentation supporting the Escrow/Impound Overdraft ( {$7300.00} ) 3. obtain itemized details and descriptions for fees and corporate advances shown in the payoff statement 4. ask whether any fees can be waived or reduced to support closing However, New American Fundings communication has been extremely poor. I repeatedly can not be routed to the correct department or decision-maker, and I have been given inconsistent information. I also received responses that did not address my documentation requests. In one email response, they referenced a Qualified Written Request/Notice of Error/Request for Information, even though I did not submit a request with that heading, and they stated they only accept these requests by mail.This situation is causing significant stress and delays and puts my scheduled closing at risk.

I am requesting that New American Funding : Provide the complete loan transaction history/ledger showing how payments, escrow, fees, and deferred balance were applied 3. Provide a written breakdown/calculation supporting the Payment Deferred Balance of {$35000.00}, including what months/amounts make up this balance 4. Provide escrow account history supporting the Escrow/Impound Overdraft of {$7300.00}, including dates/amounts of tax and insurance disbursements 5. Provide an itemized breakdown and explanation of the payoff line items including : Outstanding Fees, Recoverable Corporate Advances, Late Charges, and PMI if applicable 6. Assign a case manager/escalation contact who can communicate clearly and respond promptly due to my time-sensitive closing schedule Key dates ( include these ) Payoff statement dated : XX/XX/XXXX Closing scheduled : XX/XX/XXXX Under contract date : XX/XX/XXXX Inspection date : XX/XX/XXXX Multiple calls/emails attempted : ( several days ) I am not requesting forgiveness of valid principal. I am requesting prompt, accurate documentation, transparent breakdowns, and appropriate escalation support so my closing is not delayed due to the servicers lack of communication. 
This is not a new issue. I have experienced ongoing communication failures with New American Funding for approximately the last three years. When I have been asked to provide documentation, I have repeatedly been sent document request links that were already expired or would expire before I was able to upload documents. When I notified them the links were expired, I was told I missed the deadline and that I could have mailed the documents in, even when ( 1 ) I was not properly informed that the documents were being requested in the first place and/or ( 2 ) the document request was sent after the deadline date they referenced. This pattern has created repeated delays, confusion, and unfair blame placed on me as the borrower.

I have consistently attempted to comply with requests and communicate, but due to New American Fundings poor document request process and lack of routing to the appropriate department, I have been unable to reliably resolve issues or obtain accurate information.

In XXXX my mortgage payments went up XXXX XXXX with no advance notice. The payments went from {$1000.00} to {$1400.00}. When I called and inquired about the increase, I was informed that a rise in my insurance " automatically triggered fees '' for managing my escrow. When going over these " fees '' I learned I was being charged monthly for things I had not authorized. I at that time stated I wanted to close my escrow. I was informed that in order to do so I would need to cancel my insurance and obtain my own coverage. I was advised that once I did so, I would need to upload proof of the policy to the Pennymac website ( I have this recorded ). I was instructed that I would have to pay {$1200.00} to zero out a shortage on the escrow account in order to close it. I was advised that after zeroing out the shortage and obtaining my own insurance my total monthly payment would be {$750.00}. I attempted to pay my monthly payment and the shortage multiple times over about a XXXX week period. I spent hours on the phone with Pennymac trying to resolve this. I obtained a new insurance policy through XXXX ( effective XX/XX/XXXX ) and canceled the policy through XXXX ( ending XX/XX/XXXX ). Pennymac kept telling me it would " take another day or XXXX '' to get everything in order. I uploaded my new policy information from XXXX to the Pennymac website, as I was instructed by the Pennymac rep. When I called XXXX days later, I was advised that they had cut a check to XXXX XXXX for the annual cost of the plan, which I had already paid XXXX for the policy. They said that created an additional shortage I would have to pay. After multiple more hours on the phone, I was told that the check hadn't left their facility yet and they would pull it and cancel it ( I have this recorded ). They said they would call me the following day to confirm. The following day they called and advised they were unable to pull it " because it was in a group with other checks '' so they called XXXX and requested reimbursement once received. They told me they would have to wait until that reimbursement was received until they could move forward. I again requested a XXXX contact me, as my payment was going to show as late or missed. 

After finally threatening to sue the company, I was told that a supervisor would give me a call. I was also told that I am not allowed to record conversations I have with the company ( I have their representative recorded saying this multiple times ) even after I explained that I am in Arkansas, which is a one-party consent state. I do have my calls recorded. XXXX days later a supervisor did finally reach out to me. She advised that at this point, my account would have qualified for closing my escrow, but that I had a payment over 30 days late. She was referring to the current payment, which as of the time of my call with her, was 1 day over 30 days late when I had been attempting to pay it for the past few weeks. After talking with her over a couple day period, she stated that she would take the payment for the shortage in the escrow and the payment for the full payment of the XXXX payment ( which I agreed to because the old insurance was still active during that month ) and that she would then resubmit my appeal for closing my escrow. She promised she would personally call me back that Friday to advise me of the outcome. I told her I just wanted to make sure that it wasn't going to run over into another " late payment ''. She said that she would call to make sure. She called later that evening and confirmed she would call me in a few days. I never received a call back from her. I called multiple times and was told she was " busy ''. Eventully another rep told me that a " letter had been scheduled to go out on the XXXX ( of XXXX at this point ) advising of the determination of closing the escrow ''. She said that at that point I would be able to move forward with everything. I asked her to verify if they were closing it, and she said that I would have to wait for the letter, but that my account showed it was eligible to close the escrow ( I have this call recorded ). I just went online to view the letter and it was a denial citing the following reasons : 1 ) One or more 30 day deliquncy in the prior XXXX  months.

2 ) The escrow balance on your loan is negative 3 ) Minimum current and/or original loan to value ( LTV ) ratio was not met 4 ) Minimum FICO score was not met To address these points ; 1 ) I have had this mortgage for XXXX years with no missed or late payments. The first and only late payment was caused by Pennymac delaying this process for weeks. I attempted to pay the total shortage and mortgage payment over a dozen times on the phone over the prior weeks leading up to the account showing 1 day over the late period. 

XXXX ) The escrow balance is negative due to the extra ( unauthorized ) insurance check being sent to XXXX by Pennymac. This was their error and they stated they have already arranged reimbursement with XXXX. I have already paid my XXXX policy out of pocket. I also received a refund check from the old insurance company, which I have not cashed and have offered numerous times to provide to Pennymac. If they would advise what amount is still negative ( minus the new check they wrote to XXXX ), I would be more than happy to pay that immediately. 

XXXX ) My loan has {$150000.00} remaining. My last appraisal was around XXXX XXXXXXXX. It more than qualifies. It also qualified to have the mortgage insurance dropped a year ago. This claim is absolutely ridiculous.

4 ) My FICO score now is higher than it was when I got the original loan. It has also been affected by Pennymac stating I now have a " late payment '' even though I have recordings of myself trying to pay it numerous times over the prior weeks. 

I was told I could wait to make any further payments until this matter was resolved ( recorded ). I will make a payment for the actual amount owed for XXXX today ( {$750.00} ) but I will not pay for an insurance policy that has already been canceled and I will not pay for another insurance policy that I have already paid the provider directly. I have already paid the shortages to a XXXX balance ( other than their check they erroneously cut and have arranged reimbursement that I am not a party to ). I will no longer pay their " fees '' for handling the escrow. At this point I will take this to litigation if not resolved immediately. I have already ( at the instruction of Pennymac ) obtained and paid for a new insurance policy independently. I have already cancelled the old insurance policy. I have already paid the shortages on the escrow. The only " valid '' reason for denial is the " late payment '' which was caused by Pennymac themselves. They are attempting to force me to stay in escrow just so they can collect fees. This is an absolutely scam of a company.

complaint_what_happened: Dyck-ONeal , Inc. is unlawfully attempting to enforce a time-barred debt through threats of foreclosure on a deed of trust where the underlying promissory note became unenforceable no later than XXXX. The last payment on the loan was made in XX/XX/XXXX, and no subsequent payments or written acknowledgments have occurred. Under California law, the applicable statute of limitations for enforcement of a written note ( Code of Civil Procedure 337 ) or negotiable instrument ( Commercial Code 3118 ) has long since expired. Furthermore, California case law ( Bank of America v. La Jolla Group II, 129 Cal.App.4th 706 ( 2005 ) ) makes clear that once enforcement of the debt is time-barred, the power of sale under the deed of trust is likewise extinguished. Despite this, Dyck-ONeal and its counsel, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, have continued to issue written threats of non-judicial foreclosure in correspondence dated as recently as XX/XX/XXXX, pressuring the homeowner to submit loss mitigation applications and threatening imminent sale without any valid legal basis. This conduct is deceptive, unfair, and unlawful under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( 15 U.S.C. 1692 et seq. ) and Californias Rosenthal Act ( Civil Code 17881788.33 ). Dyck-ONeals continued threats to pursue foreclosure on a stale, extinguished debt mislead the consumer regarding their legal obligations, violate prohibitions against collection of time-barred debts, and cause significant emotional distress and financial harm. I request the CFPB investigate Dyck-O'Neals ongoing practices and take appropriate enforcement action.

complaint_what_happened:
We are requesting immediate CFPB intervention to stop a wrongful foreclosure scheduled for XX/XX/XXXX. Our servicer ( XXXX XXXX ) mishandled our loss-mitigation application, failed to send required notices, withheld insurance repair funds for over XXXX years, and placed us in foreclosure while we were actively told we were in loss-mitigation review. We have repeatedly tried to resolve this with the servicer. We had been told we were in loss mitigation for quite sometime due to the insurance claim but there were delays on the merge of XXXX XXXX and Flagstar so that was the reason we hadn't received the updated loss mitigation documents.On XX/XX/XXXX, we sent a formal hardship letter and demand for release of insurance funds to multiple departments, including Loss Mitigation, XXXX XXXX, and Executive Escalation. Despite this, no response has been received. 

Violations & Summary of Issues : Failure to Send Required Mortgage Statements & Notices ( 12 C.F.R. 1026.41 ) We have not received ANY mortgage statements or foreclosure notices because the servicer repeatedly mailed documents to a vacant property, despite being notified many times that the property was uninhabitable. We provided our rental address and my mothers permanent address ( XXXX residence ), but they continued using the wrong address. They admitted notices were returned undeliverable yet still proceeded with foreclosure. 

Dual Tracking Foreclosing While Loss-Mitigation Was Active ( 12 C.F.R. 1024.41 ( f ), ( g ) ) We were told multiple times we were already in loss mitigation and waiting for documents to be mailed. No documents ever arrived. Instead, we were suddenly informed by third parties that our home was set for foreclosure. When we called, we were again told we were in active loss mitigation review, yet foreclosure activity continued. 

Failure to Provide Loss-Mitigation Documents Within Required Timeframes ( 12 C.F.R. 1024.41 ( b ) ( 2 ) ) Every prior loss-mitigation package they sent had a XXXX deadline and required multiple signatures and notarization. They knowingly sent them to the vacant property, causing us to miss deadlines and lose beneficial terms. We repeatedly requested documents to be sent to my mothers verified address, and they agreed, but still continued sending to the wrong location. 

Forced to Sign Loan Modification Under Duress The only modification they allowed us to sign was during a prior foreclosure where we had only XXXX days before the sale. We were told we would lose the home if we did not sign immediately. The terms were extremely unfavorable : Increased our monthly payment amount, Interest rate increased from approx. 4.5 % to 7.5 %, Loan term increased to XXXX years, added Corporate advances, legal fees, and attorney fees. We requested information to what the corporate advances, legal fees and attorney fees were added and never received any information regarding it. We also requested the XXXX be terminated as we had to show equity over principal to have it removed. Never received an update for that either. 
We were told loan mods are meant to make payments affordablenot worse. We were also promised the release of the insurance funds upon signature, but that has yet to happen. We had requested the terms prior to this one that were lowering the payment, interest rate and term but were denied with no reasoning. 

Failure to Release Insurance Repair Funds ( Loss Draft Funds ) for XXXX Years ( 12 C.F.R. 1024.17 ) Our home suffered an insured loss. XXXX XXXX approved the claim but partially paid. We signed an affidavit committing to repair the home. 
Flagstar/XXXX XXXXXXXX has STILL not released repair funds because : they continue demanding work match the scopes 100 % that are outdated ( XXXX old ) and written by a contractor who was fired, they refuse to accept the updated public adjuster/contractors scope, they insist on inspections but repeatedly schedule them without showing up knowing that no additional work had been completed since the last inspection. 

The " inspector '' that comes out doesn't speak XXXX and isn't the person who actually writes the inspection report. We were told, they just look at the photos taken to write the report. We asked how someone would be able to see the significant amount of work completed just by the photos with no explanation. 

Because they withheld funds, we were unable to repair the home, unable to move back in, and forced to continue paying rent. This directly caused the default and we have explained this multiple times that we can not afford rent plus mortgage. 

XXXX ( XXXX ) XXXX ( 12 C.F.R. 1024.37 ) Because the home can not be repaired any further without funds the servicer is holding, we can not obtain outside insurance. The servicer placed extremely expensive force-placed insurance, further increasing our payments and worsening hardship and doesn't cover anything such as personal belongings. Due to the servicer holding funds for over 3 years, our property has sat vacant and has been subjected to burglarization and damage. 

Failure to Maintain Accurate Contact Information Despite updating our phone number and email for over 9 months, they keep using an old number and an incorrect email. We could not regain access to the loan portal or receive verification codes due to their failure to update our contact info. This prevented us from submitting documents and accessing our account.
