sub_issue: Trying to communicate with the company to fix an issue related to modification, forbearance, short sale, deed-in-lieu, bankruptcy, or foreclosure
complaint_what_happened:
b'To Whom It May Concern,

Following the filing of my second complaint, LoanCare has blocked access to online payments without explanation (see attached screenshot dated XXXX XXXX). This may be due to the XXXX XXXX  foreclosure notice, or it may reflect retaliatory behavior in response to my CFPB filings. Either way, I am now unable to make voluntary payments should my situation change  despite being in a confirmed forbearance plan from XXXX XXXX through XXXX XXXX0, which LoanCare has already acknowledged. My account is current, and there is no delinquency that would justify this restriction. In fact, LoanCare explicitly stated in their XXXX XXXX response to the CFPB that I am in active forbearance, yet their actions directly contradict that acknowledgment. I request that online payment access be immediately restored, along with written clarification of my account status.

LoanCares XXXX XXXX response includes, as an attachment, a letter dated XXXX XXXX, which they describe as an acknowledgment of my forbearance extension request. However, this letter was never posted to my online account  not in the Document Center under Letters or Other  and it was never mailed to me. I only became aware of it because they included it in their CFPB response. Upon reviewing my account, the only letters actually posted dated XXXX XXXX  were Missed Payment notices, which falsely stated that I had not made my XXXX  and XXXX  payments  despite being in an active forbearance plan. One of those letters even threatened foreclosure if payment was not received by XXXX XXXX. This clearly shows that LoanCare was not recognizing my approved forbearance at the time. See attached letters.

These actions may have resulted from LoanCare deleting or overriding my forbearance history after I submitted a request for an FHA extension on XXXX XXXX. This raises serious concerns about recordkeeping errors, misrepresentation of my account status, and potential servicing violations. I request that LoanCare explain why I received foreclosure threats and delinquency notices during an active forbearance period, and why the so-called acknowledgment letter dated XXXX XXXX  which they included in their CFPB response  was never posted to my online account or mailed to me directly. If this letter was intended to confirm receipt or approval of my forbearance extension request, it should have been disclosed at the time, not retroactively attached to a regulatory complaint. This lack of transparency further undermines trust and raises questions about their internal recordkeeping and communication practices. See attached screenshot dated XXXXXXXX XXXX.

I also want to note that I did receive one letter dated XXXX XXXX, which was mailed and received on XXXX XXXX. However, the information it contained was already known to me and did not address any of the substantive or punitive issues I raised in my prior communications. It also failed to indicate which of my letters or emails it was responding to, making it impossible to determine whether my concerns were even reviewed. It appeared to be a generic summary rather than a meaningful response, and it failed to resolve or even acknowledge the core concerns outlined in my letters and reaffirmation.

Additionally, LoanCare has never responded to any of my letters or emails. Ive lost count of how many times Ive reached out, but I have received automated acknowledgment notices stating that additional research time is needed. These notices offer no meaningful answers, and no one from LoanCare has followed up with a resolution. I have physical letters from LoanCare that validate this pattern of vague, non-substantive responses, but I currently do not have access to a scanner to upload them. Their responses are consistently evasive, as if they are intentionally withholding information or attempting to steer me toward decisions that could be financially harmful  even though I am protected under FHA guidelines. This lack of transparency and accountability is unacceptable. I am requesting a formal resolution in writing, as LoanCares communications have repeatedly misled me or contradicted their own statements, creating confusion and undermining trust in their servicing practices.

Furthermore, I reaffirmed my unchanged hardship within the 90-day window required by FHA guidelines, which means no new assessment or application was warranted. This reaffirmation was emailed on XXXX XXXX. LoanCare already has in its possession my prior year W-2, layoff letter, and EDD award letter, all of which were submitted in my initial Loss Mitigation Application (LMA) on XXXX XXXX to secure protection for the period of XXXX XXXX through XXXX XXXX, which has not expired. According to HUD Handbook 4000.1, when a borrower reaffirms an unchanged hardship, the servicer is not required to conduct a new financial assessment. This policy is intended to ensure continuity of assistance and prevent unnecessary delays or documentation burdens. LoanCares failure to honor this reaffirmation and its apparent disregard for FHA servicing guidelines may constitute a violation of federal policy.

Additionally, I recently noticed a mortgage plan detail in my online account that includes a notice stating, you have been approved (see attached screenshot dated XXXX). However, to date, I have not received any formal letter confirming this approval. This mirrors the same pattern I experienced with my initial forbearance, where the approval was reflected online before any official letter was mailed or posted. This delay in documentation creates confusion and undermines my ability to make informed decisions about my account. I request that LoanCare immediately provide written confirmation of this mortgage plan approval, including the terms and effective dates, and ensure that all future approvals are properly documented and disclosed in a timely manner.

I am also submitting this update to clarify that the XXXX in interest allegedly capitalized on XXXX XXXX XXXX, was not added to my online loan balance, yet was reported to credit bureaus  resulting in an inflated loan balance of XXXX My online account showed a balance of XXXX (reflecting XXXX minus a XXXX late fee paid with my August payment) and excludes any interest capitalization. This discrepancy violates both FHA servicing guidelines  specifically HUD Handbook 4000.1, Section III.A.2.k.v(B)(2), which states: The Mortgagee must not capitalize late charges, fees, penalties, or interest  and the Fair Credit Reporting Act (FCRA), which requires accurate and verifiable reporting. As a result of this inflated balance being reported to credit bureaus, my credit score dropped, causing additional financial harm during an already difficult period. I request a full breakdown of how this interest was calculated, why it was added to my credit file, and immediate correction of both my loan records and credit reporting. Therefore, LoanCare cannot legally add unpaid interest to my balance as reflected on my credit reports.

I want to be clear: this is my forever home. I have no intention of selling, refinancing, or transferring it. Ive stated this repeatedly in prior written communications to LoanCare. The only reason Im able to write all of this is because Im currently stuck on my computer looking for a job  not because I have time or energy to spare. Im doing everything I can to protect my home and navigate this hardship. The FHA offers many programs to assist homeowners like myself, and Ive followed every step to request help. I simply ask that LoanCare honor my forbearances and forbearance extension requests now and in the near future, correct their records, and stop sending foreclosure threats that contradict their own statements. I do not have time  emotionally, financially, or practically  to deal with illegal foreclosure threats or attempts.'

CFPB Complaint # XXXX and XXXX I am requesting this case be reopened and escalated, as Selene Finances response fails to address the core issues raised : Selene induced the default by canceling autopay after a single failed transaction without notifying me electronically, and did not offer a reasonable opportunity to cure. 

They continued sending notices to the wrong address even after I was clearly corresponding from my new address. I received no valid acceleration or foreclosure notice at my actual location. 

Their payment portal failed, and I was locked out of my account for weeks yet they continued charging default interest at 18 %, which I dispute as unauthorized. 

I offered to pay the full amount of the missed payments under protest, and Selene refused unless I paid inflated amounts with legal fees and retroactive interest.

Selene admits I made a partial reinstatement payment of ~ {$14000.00}, yet they still keep the account in foreclosure, showing no intent to resolve this in good faith.

I am in the process of refinancing and intend to pay off the loan in full however, I am requesting CFPB oversight to ensure no junk fees or unearned interest are capitalized into the payoff quote.

I respectfully request that the CFPB keep this case open until Selene : Removes default interest charges added under disputed servicing practices ; Accepts full reinstatement or provides a clean payoff quote without retroactive fees ; Removes foreclosure status from public records.

complaint_what_happened:
ecipient or Organization Name ], I am writing to file a formal complaint regarding the mishandling of my mortgage account by my lender, Mr. Cooper ( Nationstar Mortgage ), related to a forbearance arrangement that was initiated due to a federally declared disaster in my area. 

In XX/XX/XXXX, I was placed into a XXXX-month disaster forbearance plan after a major storm affected my region. This forbearance was approved by Mr. Cooper. In XX/XX/XXXX, at the conclusion of the initial forbearance period, I contacted Mr. Cooper to request a repayment plan or loan workout to resume making regular payments. 

Instead of immediately processing the repayment plan, I was informed that an application had to be submitted, and in the meantime, I was placed into an additional forbearance. I was under the impression that this was part of the necessary process to obtain an approved repayment plan, and I acted in good faith to follow the lenders instructions. 

To my shock, at the end of XX/XX/XXXX, I received notice that Mr. Cooper denied my workout request and stated that my only options were foreclosure or a short sale. This came as a complete surprise, as I believed I was actively engaged in resolving the matter and preparing to resume payments. 

In order to prevent foreclosure proceedings and preserve my home, I brought my loan current and reinstated the mortgage. However, I was required to pay legal fees tied to foreclosure actions that had already been initiated without my knowledge and while I believed I was protected under an active forbearance. 

After reinstating the loan, I requested a refund of those legal fees, but Mr. Cooper denied it. They later informed me that my forbearance had been canceled at the end of XX/XX/XXXX, something I was never notified about nor consented to. I received no written or verbal communication from Mr. Cooper about this cancellation. 

I believe that Mr. Cooper acted in bad faith and potentially in violation of consumer protection laws by : 1. Failing to clearly communicate the status or termination of my forbearance.

2. Initiating foreclosure proceedings while I was in what I believed to be an active forbearance.

3. Failing to provide an accessible or timely repayment plan option as part of loss mitigation.

4. Charging legal fees for a foreclosure I was not aware of and not offering any path to dispute those charges. 

I am requesting the following : A full investigation into how my forbearance and repayment request were handled ; A refund of the legal fees associated with the foreclosure action taken without my informed knowledge ; Confirmation of all relevant dates regarding the forbearance status, reinstatement, and communications ; Assurance that my account will be accurately reported to credit bureaus with no negative marks due to this situation. 

I am also seeking legal counsel and will escalate this matter to all appropriate regulatory agencies. I would prefer to resolve this issue amicably, but I am prepared to pursue all remedies available under law.

complaint_what_happened:
On XX/XX/XXXX, and XX/XX/XXXX, my wife spoke with representatives from New Rez regarding our mortgage payment schedule. Both conversations took place on recorded lines and lasted approximately 15 minutes and 20 minutes, respectively. During these calls, New Rez agents explicitly informed my wife that a mortgage payment due on the 1st of a given month could be paid as late as the 1st of the following month without being reported to credit bureaus as a late payment. Specifically, they stated that New Rez does not report payments as late until the 2nd of the following month. 

Relying on this information, we made our XX/XX/XXXX, mortgage payment on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXXwithin the timeframe that their agents confirmed would not result in negative credit reporting. However, we later discovered that New Rez reported our payment as 30+ days late to the credit bureaus, which caused our credit scores to drop by approximately 60 points each.

Upon noticing the negative impact on our credit scores, we immediately contacted New Rez to dispute the inaccurate reporting. During this conversation, they acknowledged that an investigation could be initiated but required us to submit a written request via fax or mail. We have complied with this request and are currently awaiting their response. 

New Rez has confirmed that they will review the call recordings as part of their investigation, but they have not guaranteed that they will reverse the negative reporting even if they find that their agents provided incorrect information. This is deeply concerning, as their misrepresentation directly influenced our financial decisions and resulted in serious harm to our creditworthiness. 

Furthermore, this situation appears predatory and possibly discriminatory. My wife, who handled these calls, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX. There is concern that she may have been provided misleading information in an intentional effort to damage our credit standing, potentially preventing us from refinancing with another lender. Whether the misinformation was a result of XXXX XXXX XXXX systemic negligence, the result has been financially devastating to us as consumers. 

We are requesting that the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ) investigate New Rezs actions and require them to : Correct the inaccurate late payment reporting and restore our credit scores. 
Implement safeguards to ensure borrowers receive accurate payment guidance from customer service representatives. 
Provide transparency regarding their investigation process and criteria for reversing wrongful credit reporting. 
Determine whether there is any pattern of discriminatory practices in the companys interactions with borrowers. 
We appreciate the CFPBs assistance in ensuring fair and lawful treatment of consumers.

complaint_what_happened:
In late XXXX, I began working with mortgage one inc 's loss and mitigation department on applying for a loan modification. I was told it was required of me to make three trial payments before I would be approved for this modification. I proceeded to make those payments in XXXX XXXX and XXXX, at which point I was approved and issued the paperwork to complete the loan modification. However, there were several errors included in the initial documentation that was issued to me including, but not limited to.. and incorrect interest rate a principal amount balanced of nearly XXXX more than the actual balance, conflicting dates, and multiple new payment amounts. 
I made multiple phone calls regarding this issue before finally talking to a supervisor who escalated my case. I was finally contacted by the underwriter who actually drafted this initial documentation. I spoke with her for over a half hour has she continually argued with me very rudely might I add that there were no errors in the paperwork that was issued. After arguing with me and having me send pictures of the documentation proving that the numbers on her screen that were matching up with the numbers on my screen as I was signed into my online account, we're not matching up with the numbers that was on the actual documentation that was issued. At this point she concurred she would need to draft new documents and have them reissued. Several weeks passed and I still hadn't heard anything nor had I received this new paperwork. It took me several more calls requesting call back before I finally had an escalated again to a supervisor who instructed me that it was now being required that I provide a quick claim deed because they were alleging that my ex-wife 's name was on the deed. 
I argued very emphatically but this was not true and and explains that it would be impossible for me to provide such a document because the register of deeds wouldn't be able to produce such a document without my ex-wife 's name being on the deed. I was continuously told multiple times from multiple case managers that this was the requirement and I would need to provide this document that was impossible to produce. Finally my attorney recommended I offer up the option of sending mortgage one a copy of my divorce documents which clearly stated my ex-wife had no interest in the home. Ultimately this would equal a quick claim deed because the purpose of a quit claim deed is to terminate any interest in a property and the divorce decree clearly showed that she had none. 
Another supervisor that I finally spoke with a a week or so later stated that the divorce documents should surprise and had me emailed them over. I was told once they were reviewed that I would receive a call or the new paperwork would just be issued and to keep a lookout for the new documentation. A few more weeks went by and I still haven't heard anything nor had I received this new documentation and so I called loss/mitigation again, and I was told that the divorce documents would not suffice and that I would indeed need to submit a quit claim deed. That day I went down to the register of deeds here in XXXX XXXX and explain the situation and they printed me off a copy of my deed and lo and behold, my ex-wife 's name WAS NOT ON IT! 
I called mortgage one to inform them of this as I was standing in the register of deeds office and the clerk was laughing as I was explaining to the mortgage one case manager that they were requiring me to produce a document that was literally impossible to provide. 
I was asked to send a copy of the deed which I had just received from the register of deeds office for review. Shortly thereafter, I received a phone call informing me that they could now proceed with drafting the new documentation to be reissued. Several more weeks went by and I still haven't received this documentation so I called to check on the situation again and ended up having to request to speak to a supervisor again, and have my case escalated again ... Only to be told that it was in the system that it was still awaiting reissuance and that I should be expecting it within a couple weeks. When I finally received the documentation and had an opportunity to go over it and confirm the numbers were correct, I realized that it did not make the best financial sense to go through with the loan modification, but rather to do a deferment. 
An early XXXX, the supervisor that I spoke to who instructed me to send the copy of the divorce documents over and that they should have sufficed, explain to me that when I received the new paperwork if I was not happy with it that I still have the option to do a 6-month deferment because they had enough money from the payments that I had sent over the last few months to still do a deferment. 
Now that I had a chance to go over the numbers once I had received the corrected documentation, they are telling me that I am beyond the 6-month window and can no longer qualify for the 6 month deferment. 
I was hung up on multiple times including twice by the same supervisor when trying to calmly explain the situation and kept being rudely interrupted. I was hung up on after calling her out for being rude and not letting me speak and explain the situation.. that one of their employees, a supervisor at that, was the one who had told me that once I received the documentation there was enough money in my account from the payments I had made to loss mitigation where they could initiate a request to underwriting for an exception to the rule based on the grounds mortgage one was accountable for the delay and the paperwork that was the reason for my being beyond the 6-month window. After being hung up on the second time by that supervisor I called the customer helpline and instructed them I was prepared to file a complaint with the cfpb as well as corporate, and was put on hold for a Time.. when the customer Care Rep came back on the line, I was told that my case was being accelerated and that meant that one person was going to be handling my case now throughout the duration of the process and that I should be expecting an email. That email came two days later in my spam folder, at which point I immediately responded to it. The email stated that he was assuming I was questioning the new documentation and that he sees that it has been reissued and received by me and everything should be fine now and if I had any questions to contact customer care. I immediately responded to this and explain to this person that know that did not answer my questions that did not resolve the situation and that it was not resolved. Now I can't get anybody on the phone and I have until the XXXX to sign these documents or they're going to foreclose on my home. I have full custody of my XXXX XXXX XXXX  daughter and it is just her and I residing in this home. 
If multiple people in their department hadn't dropped the ball and the original documents that were issued wouldn't have been full of errors and I wouldn't have had to wait for new documents to be reissued and then jump through the hoops of being required to request a quick claim deed which I could not physically produce, to waiting another few weeks for the new documentation to be mailed, we wouldn't be in the situation and I would have been able to do the deferment long before I was out of the 6-month window I am only beyond the 6-month window because of the incompetence of the mortgage one lost and mitigation department. 
They have not been polite they have not been helpful, and it seems they are just trying to push my home into foreclosure. I can not afford to have my attorney handle this so I'm hoping I can find some resolution through this complaint since I can't get anywhere over the phone with these people.

sub_issue: Changes in loan terms during or after closing
complaint_what_happened:
I applied for a mortgage loan with Lima One Capital in XX/XX/XXXX to purchase a property at XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX From the outset, I provided all binding information in the signed application, including my down payment and loan structure. Based on the original quote, I paid the required fees and moved forward in good faith. 

XX/XX/XXXX Received initial loan quote with locked interest rate of 6.483 % and origination fee options ( {$750.00} or {$1700.00} with credit ). Based on these terms, I agreed to proceed. 

XXXX Paid {$1200.00} appraisal fee and {$350.00} inspection fee. 

XX/XX/XXXX Appraisal and inspection were completed. 

XXXX Repeatedly requested confirmation of loan terms as appraisal and underwriting wrapped up. My questions went unanswered for several days. 

XX/XX/XXXX Instead of confirmation, I was presented with materially different loan terms : higher interest rates ( 7.016 % ), higher origination fees ( {$1700.00} ), and altered loan-to-value ( 75 % instead of 70 % ). These changes did not match the signed application or original quote. 

Throughout this period, closing was delayed multiple times, forcing me to extend international travel, cancel arrangements, and lose business opportunities. 

As a result of these changes and delays, I have incurred the following direct financial losses : {$800.00} unreimbursed appraisal fee ( only {$400.00} refunded after the lender determined a mistaken condo certification fee ). 

{$350.00} inspection fee, fully lost. 

{$990.00} earnest money deposit, forfeited because the shifting loan terms and delays prevented timely closing. 

I believe the loan was not handled in good faith. The final terms materially differed from what was originally quoted and agreed upon, despite all binding information being provided upfront. My repeated efforts to avoid reaching this stage were ignored, and I was only presented with changes at the last minute.

Re : XXXX XXXX Demand & RESPA Notice Unfair and Deceptive Mortgage Servicing Practices To Whom It May Concern : I am the borrower on the mortgage loan secured by XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. I am writing pursuant to XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  to demand relief for unfair and deceptive acts committed by NewRez LLC XXXX Shellpoint Mortgage Servicing ( Shellpoint ) in connection with servicing this loan. The XXXX XXXX Notice dated XX/XX/XXXX, identifies factual inaccuracies and omissions. 

Facts Shellpoint received preservation payments that were recognized in my bankruptcy proceedings in from XXXX through XXXX yet continues to assert I defaulted inclusive of this period. 
Shellpoint withheld a required appraisal for approximately XXXX  months before negotiating loss mitigation, in violation of federal servicing standards and industry duties of good faith. 
Shellpoint offered a conditional modification/settlement of $ XXXX that I accepted but they failed to honor. 

Per the XXXX Right-To-Cure notification, there is {$1700.00} as an unapplied balance, further evidence of misapplied preservation payments and other accounting issues. 
Shellpoint referred my loan to outside counsel ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) to collect on disputed amounts, misrepresenting my account status. This notice is defective because it implies personal liability on a discharged debt in violation of Bankruptcy Code - Discharge Injunction Violation ( 11 U.S.C. 524 ( a ) ).

Key Errors in Shellpoints Right to Cure Notice 1. 1 ) Failure to account for courtapproved preservation payments. 
I made preservation payments in the amount of {$2300.00} per month from XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX pursuant to orders entered in my bankruptcy case. Your Right to Cure Notice asserts nonpayment of every monthly installment from XX/XX/XXXX through XX/XX/XXXX. By ignoring XXXX years of preservation payments recognized by the bankruptcy court, the Notice materially overstates the arrears and is misleading. 
XXXX. XXXX ) Servicing transfer/recordkeeping deficiency. 
Shellpoint requested I supply proof of the XXXX payments because it did not have the complete prior servicers history ( See enclosures : Years XXXX and XXXX are missing in their entirety ). After I directed Shellpoint to the bankruptcy court docket, Shellpoint still forwarded inaccurate loan histories and noticed default for the entire period. This demonstrates a failure to maintain accurate loan records following a servicing transfer and to conduct a reasonable investigation of my dispute. 

XXXX. XXXX ) Escrow and unapplied funds discrepancies. 
The Notice reflects multiple stepups in escrow amounts and an unapplied balance of ( {$1700.00} ) held in suspense. Shellpoint has not explained why those funds were not applied to reduce arrears or principal. Without a contemporaneous escrow analysis and a full suspense account history, Shellpoint can not lawfully demand inflated arrears or declare default based on amounts not actually due. 

Errors Under Massachusetts and Federal Law Massachusetts law ( G.L. c. XXXX, XXXX ) requires strict compliance with default/Right to Cure requirements ; misstated or misleading arrears figures render a notice defective. Further, federal servicing regulations require accurate account maintenance, timely investigation and response to borrower disputes, and goodfaith lossmitigation handling. As with XXXX XXXX XXXX Shellpoint in XXXX for mishandled servicing & foreclosure practices, there is already precedent for misconduct. 

Violations Unfair and deceptive acts under XXXX. XXXX XXXX, c and XXXX ; Violations of 12 U.S.C. 2605 ( RESPA ) and 12 C.F.R. 1024.35, 1024.36 ; and 1024.41 ; Violations of 12 C.F.R. 1024.38 ( servicing policies and procedures ) ; Bad faith conduct similar to conduct found unlawful in the XXXX XXXX Attorney Generals settlement with Shellpoint. 

Demand for Immediate Action Provide a written correction and reconciliation of my account that credits all preservation payments made between XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX ; Produce a complete payment history/transaction ledger from XX/XX/XXXX to present, including suspense account activity, unapplied funds, and application dates ; Produce all lossmitigation logs and the appraisal order/receipt timeline, along with all communications with prior servicers ; Provide escrow analyses supporting each escrow amount reflected in the Right to Cure Notice and correct any overcharges ; Issue a corrected Right to Cure Notice, if Shellpoint continues to contend a default exists, reflecting accurate arrears and dates ; Cease any foreclosure activity and collection efforts based on inaccurate figures until the above corrections are completed. 

Response Deadline This is both a XXXX XXXXXXXX demand and a RESPA Notice of Error/Request for Information. Please provide a written response within 30 days under XXXX XXXX and within the timelines specified by 12 C.F.R. 1024.35 and 1024.36. Failure to timely correct and respond will result in the pursuit of all remedies, including suit for damages, attorneys fees, and multiple damages for willful violations. 

Enclosures First and second request for Loan History from Shellpoint Mortgage Servicing Shellpoint Mortgage Timeline from the Compliance Department noting the history of requests for an accurate Loan History and Declinations of Loan Modification Requests on XX/XX/XXXX and XX/XX/XXXX. 
Debt Collection Notice from XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX United States Bankruptcy XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX attached to Affirmation of Debtor Opposition to Lift Stay Motion Exhibit A. 

If Shellpoint fails to make a reasonable written offer of settlement within 30 days, I will file suit seeking multiple damages, costs, and attorneys fees as authorized by XXXX XXXX, XXXX. 

The files with the loan history ( first and second requests ) from Shellpoint Mortgage Servicing are too large to upload, and by certified mail I have forwarded a complete history/package to your XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX all enclosures listed above. Shellpoint 's Compliance Department mailed me a timeline asserting there there are no errors or omissions, however, as I pointed out on several occasions, the years XXXX XXXX XXXX are missing in their entirety which you will note when my package is arrives. To that end, the XXXX XXXX is defective.

I inquired about taking money out of my HELOC to pay off credit card debt on XXXX with M & T Bank. I was told the terms would be XXXX XXXXXXXX 8.89 % with a payment of {$250.00} a month. I asked a couple times to be sure that the full balance owed was included in this payment. She told me it was so I moved forward with locking in the new interest rate/payment. 

I then received a bill for $ XXXX dollars. I called the bank to inquire why my bill was so much higher, the lady told me there was just one payment left of my previous HELOC and the next month my payment would go back to the agreed upon $ XXXX. I made the payment. Then I received a letter stating I had XXXX separate loans and the total of the payment was I believe $ XXXX! I spoke with the manager of the bank, after a few days they told me they can not honor the payment they quoted me over the phone.

My home was destroyed, and my insurance company issued {$100000.00} in funds. The mortgage servicer is Newrez Mortgage Servicing. 
On XX/XX/scrub> 2025, my insurance company wired the funds to Newrez, but they denied the wire. 
A physical check was then mailed to me, which I endorsed and returned to Newrez. 
They received the check on XX/XX/year>2025. 
All required documentation to process the claim was submitted by me on XX/XX/year>2025. 

On XX/XX/year>2025, Newrez claimed they mailed a partial disbursement of {$40000.00} via XXXX. However, when I contacted XXXX, they confirmed that no pickup was made from Newrez on that date. I personally paid for the XXXX label to ensure safe delivery, yet the check was never sent. 

Newrez has now told me that reissuing the check will take 35 business days, or more, despite the delay being entirely on their part. I have been without these critical funds for over a month, unable to begin necessary repairs on my home. 

I have only been allowed to speak with call center representatives and have not been provided access to anyone with the authority to correct this. complaint_what_happened:
In XXXX of XXXX, we had taken out a HELOC with BMO to replace an older LOC with BMO with a LOC with a higher limit. On XX/XX/XXXX, my spouse, XXXX made a payment to our HELOC for {$30000.00}. These were funds that we had drawn in excess from our LOC and had sitting in our checking account. Our plans had changed, so we no longer needed to borrow the {$30000.00}. She had mistakenly posted the payment as a principal only payment when her intention was to pay a Principal and interest payment, advancing our payment due date to XXXX. 

At the time we called on the XXXX of XXXX, we were showing funds available to draw. The call center person XXXX spoke to said he would fix it. The account transaction history looked strange the next couple of days. Today I checked the availability on the LOC to see that it was {$0.00}, when it should be {$24000.00} roughly. 

I spent 30 minutes on hold, then to only be told that because we made a large payment and it couldn't be verified, then there was a 10 day hold placed. That's strange because we were never notified about this hold. When I told them the funds came from BMO, they said I would need to send in verification of those funds. Those funds initially came from this LOC. Furthermore, the payment of {$30000.00} was made on XX/XX/XXXX, but the hold was until XXXX XXXX, 10 days from the date of the requested maintenance. The person on the phone never indicated a hold.

I've spent over 1.5 hours on the phone with BMO or XXXX  or whoever they are calling themselves today. I did a HELOC and I'm being forced to speak to a servicing company I didn't agree to deal with. Furthermore I'm being told that any payment made to my HELOC over {$10000.00} requires a HOLD of 10 days. This is not addressed anywhere in my loan agreement. I've asked for the documentation where I've agreed to this and noone was able or interested in answering my questions. I've lost all trust in this Financial Institution and I want a resolution ASAP.

sub_issue: An existing modification, forbearance plan, short sale, or other loss mitigation relief
complaint_what_happened:
My loan was in a COVID XXXX era default status. I applied for loss mitigation in XX/XX/XXXX. 
In XX/XX/XXXX, AmeriHome approved a loss mitigation application on my loan for a VA XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ). This offer included a Trial Payment Plan ( TPP ) dated and was dated XX/XX/XXXX. The offer contained a clear internal discrepancy : the principal-and-interest plus escrow amounts totaled {$2100.00}, but AmeriHome demanded {$2400.00}. No explanation was provided anywhere in the offer that explained or could account for the {$360.00} discrepancy in the requested payment vs the payment itemization. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, I submitted our written acceptance of the XXXX offer as instructed per the XX/XX/XXXX terms to prevent foreclosure action and I requested clarification on the amount we were expected to pay and informed amerihome that it appears there is a request for overpayment. We asked clarifications be given prior to the date of the first payment for the TPP ( XX/XX/XXXX ). AmeriHome did not respond. In a recorded call, AmeriHome informed us that TPP payments may be made any time during the month they are due, which we relied on as we awaited correction of the miscalculation. 
As XXXX approached and AmeriHome remained silent, we submitted a formal Notice of Error ( NOE ) dated XX/XX/XXXX. At that point AmeriHome had both : ( 1 ) our written acceptance, and ( 2 ) an active NOE requiring a Regulation X response. 
We tendered the full {$2400.00} through money orders in XXXX. A XXXX tracking receipt confirms AmeriHome physically received the payment on XX/XX/XXXX. The funds were unconditional and delivered during the correct month. Nothing prevented AmeriHome from cashing or applying the payment to the XXXX TPP installment. And prior to sending, we received verbal affirmation that offer was still valid, and payment could be accepted through the end of the month of XX/XX/XXXX form amerihome. 
We included an allocation instruction based on AmeriHomes own numbers and specifically stated that if our understanding was incorrect, and the total was not an inflated/ over-calculation, AmeriHome should contact us immediately. AmeriHome never contacted us, never objected, and never returned the funds. 
AmeriHomes own statements then show : The full payment was logged as PARTIAL/UNAPPLIED PAYMT on XX/XX/XXXX they had the money but did not apply it to the XXXX installment. 
On XX/XX/XXXX, AmeriHome applied the entire {$2400.00} amount against Escrow Shortage ( Taxes and Insurance ) not to the TPP, not to arrears, and not to principal or interest. 
We later learned AmeriHome had internally terminated the TPP in XXXX but never disclosed this to us. We later learned, they treated our XXXX NOE as an appeal. In XX/XX/XXXX, AmeriHome sent us an appeal decision dated XX/XX/XXXX, stating they had taken a second look and were upholding their determination, even though we had never appealed anything and our NOE was still unanswered. The letter made no sense and did not address the underlying mathematical error. 
Even more concerning, I called AmeriHome in XX/XX/XXXX after this letter was generated and the TPP canceled, and in a recorded call, AmeriHome explicitly told us the loss-mit option was still active, that the review was concluded and they sent us a full break down of the amounts and we should be getting it anytime, and that we would be receiving all the clarification we had requested. That clarification never came. In fact, it appears the appeal denial letter is the letter we were told is on the way- and it provided no such answer to our NOE or clarification on the obvious error in our loss mit offer. AmeriHome staff continued to represent that the option was open despite the fact that AmeriHome had already terminated it internally. At the time, we believed the letter was on its way that would explain how to proceed. 
Instead of receiving clarification as promised, we got a new foreclosure notice. Approximately XXXX months after secretly terminating the TPP offer, AmeriHome recorded a Notice of Default on XX/XX/XXXX, initiating a new foreclosure while : a complete loss-mit review had been approved, our written acceptance was on file, the full XXXX payment had been tendered and received on XX/XX/XXXX, AmeriHomes staff were affirmatively stating the TPP remained active, and the NOE remained unanswered.

This conduct constitutes prohibited dual-tracking under 12 C.F.R. 1024.41. 
Since the events of XX/XX/XXXX, we have repeatedly requested the exact termination date of the XXXX offer and the reason for termination. AmeriHome has never provided either. No cancellation notice was issued. No NOE response was provided, in violation of RESPA/Reg X . Only an appeal decision that made no sense in the circumstances and was not expressly stating a current status of the loss mitigation offer. 

We sent a QWR letter to the new servicer in XX/XX/XXXX right after getting a notice in late XX/XX/XXXX essentially saying we had no active loss mitigation activity. 
We never received the requested documents regarding this issue and it was deemed a request that was over board and burdensome. We made many verbal requests, those never result in action either. 

Finally, I want to be clear to the servicer/ lender that nothing in this complaint should be used by AmeriHome to terminate, delay, or negatively affect any currently pending or complete loss-mitigation reviews or protections currently in place that the VA has confirmed approving. Filing this complaint is not a withdrawal, abandonment, or refusal to cooperate. It is a request for regulatory oversight to correct prior servicing errors. Any adverse action on a pending loss-mitigation review based on this complaint would violate RESPA/Reg X and would constitute prohibited retaliation.

I am a homeowner attempting to avoid foreclosure through a mortgage modification. I have submitted multiple complete loan modification applications to my mortgage servicer, but have been repeatedly denied without clear explanations. This appears to violate the federal mortgage servicing rules that prohibit " dual tracking, '' where foreclosure is pursued while a complete application for loss mitigation is being considered. 

I am requesting : A full explanation of why my applications were denied A proper review of my most recent application A pause or reversal of any foreclosure activity until a fair review is completed I am seeking help to retain my home and believe I qualify for modification assistance. complaint_what_happened:
XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX Mr. Cooper Mortgage XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ] Subject : Urgent Complaint and Request for Personal Assistance on My Mortgage Account Dear Mr. Cooper Customer Support, I am writing to formally request immediate assistance and escalation regarding my mortgage account, which was transferred to Mr. Cooper from XXXX XXXX  . I have been attempting to get a response regarding my account, including a request for a dedicated representative to help me navigate the severe and long-standing issues caused by XXXX XXXX  negligence and errors.

As of today, my emails and calls have gone unanswered, leaving me in limbo. I am now escalating this as a formal complaint to the CFPB due to Mr. Coopers failure to provide adequate communication, transparency, or resolution. I expect a prompt response before I am forced to take further action.

Background of the Issue : XXXX Mismanagement of My Mortgage In XX/XX/XXXX, an agent from XXXX XXXX  contacted me to take me out of 18 months of forbearance. I was told that if I paid my XXXX mortgage, I would be current and that the 18 forbearance payments would be deferred to the end of my loan term. 

I made my XXXX and XXXX payments as instructed. However, my payments were never applied correctly, despite agents telling me repeatedly that I was current.

Incorrect Payment Amounts & Unauthorized Address Changes I later discovered that XXXX changed my address incorrectly 47 times between XX/XX/XXXX and the present day. These errors meant that critical documents were sent to undeliverable addresses.

Additionally, XXXX agents repeatedly gave me incorrect mortgage payment amounts, leading to my payments being categorized as partial payments and held in suspense instead of being applied to my loan. 

XXXX Admitted Their Mistakes on XX/XX/XXXX On XX/XX/XXXX, I called to begin the mortgage assumption process for my ex-husband. Instead, I was told that XXXX had failed to send the necessary paperwork for two separate mortgage adjustments.

Four agents, including supervisors, confirmed that I was not at fault and that I had been approved for a 4 % interest rate with a {$2500.00} payment in XX/XX/XXXX, but the paperwork was never sent. 

They promised to resubmit the documents within three days, but when I called back, I was stonewalled. XXXX had brought back the 18 forbearance payments without informing me and had added my last seven payments to them, classifying me as 25 months behind overnight.

Stonewalling and CFPB Violations I called over 50 times, trying to get a clear answer on how to report these issues. XXXX refused to provide a department to escalate loss mitigation complaints, despite my complex XXXX XXXX  and medical condition. 

XXXX representatives acknowledged their mistakes but refused to correct them. They blocked my mortgage adjustment, pushed me into loss mitigation under false pretenses, and then failed me in the process.

I later received a letter from XXXX Presidents Office falsely claiming that I had changed my address in XX/XX/XXXX, despite clear documentation that I never submitted an address change request.

Mortgage Transfer to Mr. Cooper XXXX Response My mortgage was transferred to Mr. Cooper, yet I have not received any assistance or response to my inquiries.

I have requested a personal representative to help sort out these issues, but no one has returned my calls or emails.

Mr. Cooper now owns the loan, which means they are responsible for addressing these violations, yet they are failing to take action.

What I Am Demanding From Mr. Cooper : Immediate Assignment of a Dedicated Representative I need a single point of contact to work through these errors and provide clear answers. No more being transferred endlessly with no resolution.

A Full Investigation Into My Mortgage History Mr. Cooper must review Flagstars mishandling of my payments, mortgage adjustments, and unauthorized address changes and provide a corrective action plan.

Account Corrections and Acknowledgment of CFPB Violations The payment discrepancies and incorrect address changes must be corrected in writing.

If Mr. Cooper refuses to address these violations, I will escalate my complaint to multiple regulatory bodies and the media.

A Formal Written Response to This Complaint I demand a formal response within 10 business days, outlining the steps Mr. Cooper will take to resolve these issues.

Final Warning I have been fighting this battle alone, with brain damage and severe complex XXXX, while recovering from a life-threatening illness. XXXX XXXX failure to respond is a direct continuation of XXXXs negligence and misconduct.

If I do not receive a response within 10 business days, I will escalate my complaint to : The CFPB The OCC State Attorney General FTC HUD Consumer advocacy groups and the media I am prepared to take legal action if necessary. I will not be ignored any longer.

I expect an immediate response. If Mr. Cooper intends to operate as a legitimate mortgage servicer, they must address gross violations of consumer protection laws and basic ethical business practices. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX loan number CC : Consumer Financial Protection Bureau ( CFPB ), Office of the Comptroller of the Currency ( OCC ), Federal Trade Commission ( FTC ), Housing and Urban Development ( HUD ), State Attorney General

Subject : Rhode Island XXXX to XXXX XXXX XXXX in Response to RESPA QWR Summary of Issue : On XX/XX/year>, Rhode Island XXXX ( RIHousing ) issued a response to my written Request for Information ( QWR ) under 12 C.F.R. 1024.36, originally submitted on XX/XX/year>. The QWR specifically sought all records concerning the {$17000.00} XXXX XXXX account ledgers, wire confirmations, disbursement instructions, compliance audit trails, and related agency reporting. 

Instead of providing the requested records, RIHousing asserted blanket objections under 12 C.F.R. 1024.36 ( f ) ( 1 ) ( ii ), claiming virtually all such records were " proprietary and confidential. '' This included refusal to provide basic account-level documentation ( such as wire transfer confirmations, disbursement ledgers, journal entries, and ARPA/agency reports XXXX, all of which are necessary to determine if the DPA funds were properly applied and disclose

d. RIHousing did not provide individualized explanations or conduct a reasonable investigation responsive to each information request.

Law/Regulation at Issue : Regulation X ( 12 C.F.R. 1024.36 ), specifically servicer obligations to make available servicing records and respond to borrower information requests regarding account application of payments and third-party funds.

Details and Impact : RIHousings response is noncompliant : Regulation X requires production of account-level, transactional, and compliance records used to answer a borrowers QWR, except for narrowly defined confidential/proprietary information.

Servicers broad objections fail to distinguish between allowable non-disclosure and required records.

The refusal undermines the ability to determine if public funds ( grant/ARPA ) were processed lawfully and if all program disclosures were proper.

This obstructive conduct stymies borrower review, accountability, and possible correction of errors or irregularities with the handling of the grant and mortgage account.

Requested Relief : Direct RIHousing to immediately produce all requested servicing, wire, and disbursement records, as required by federal law.

Conduct a regulatory review of RIHousings pattern of QWR response practices to determine if widespread noncompliance exists.

Impose corrective or enforcement measures if systemic RESPA violations are found.

Require a written explanation detailing the true application and accounting of public assistance funds on this account.

complaint_what_happened:
This letter and the attached documents provide further information regarding the potential foreclosure of my home by Navy Federal Credit Union. I am outlining the key events and providing supporting documentation for your review. 

I initially applied for forbearance in XX/XX/XXXX due to wrongful termination from my position. NFCU 's handling of my application was problematic, with repeated requests for " missing '' documentation, even though I eventually provided everything they requested. My first forbearance was finally approved in XX/XX/XXXX and lasted until XX/XX/XXXX. 

Knowing my financial hardship would continue, on XX/XX/XXXX, I proactively submitted the *exact same* application package that they had previously deemed complete and approved in XXXX. This time, they again claimed missing documentation. This was particularly frustrating as I had simply re-dated the previously accepted documents. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I submitted a completely new application package from scratch, comparing it side-by-side with the original, previously accepted package, and included a written list addressing their alleged missing items. I received no communication until XX/XX/XXXX, when they called twice in under XXXX minute at XXXX XXXX  ( on a Thursday ), leaving me only Friday to address any issues before my supposed " deadline '' of Sunday, XX/XX/XXXX. 

They then claimed my hardship letter was insufficient, stating it needed to be two separate letters. This requirement was never communicated to me either verbally or in writing during the initial application process or the subsequent attempts to renew/extend my forbearance. 

From Thursday to Monday, I was attending a conference focused on securing employment and business opportunities to overcome my financial hardship. Upon my return, I contacted Navy Federal and was informed that my home was in active foreclosure, with an auction date set for XX/XX/XXXX, at XXXX XXXX. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received the Notice of Trustee Sale under Deed of Trust dated XX/XX/XXXX. The record request which was made through XXXX indicates that the foreclosure process must have begun weeks prior, certainly before my forbearance renewal package was even properly reviewed. 

As we discussed, I believe NFCU 's actions raise serious concerns about potential bad faith and illegal foreclosure practices. Their inconsistent and ever-changing documentation requirements, coupled with the extremely short notice of the foreclosure sale, suggest they are not acting in good faith. I am particularly concerned that the foreclosure process was initiated prematurely and without proper consideration of my forbearance application. 

I have attached images that coincide with the information I have provided and can provide further documentation upon request. 

Thank you for taking the time to review this information. I look forward to discussing this matter further with you after you have had a chance to examine the documents.

sub_issue: Trying to communicate with the company to fix an issue related to modification, forbearance, short sale, deed-in-lieu, bankruptcy, or foreclosure
complaint_what_happened:
This is a very serious situation, and it's understandable that we seeking guidance. Being subjected to wrongful foreclosure, especially as a veteran, is a significant injustice. Given the complexity of the entities involved ( XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX, BofA XXXX XXXX XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX XXXX ), and the alleged insulation of the Georgia property from the sale of shares in a XXXX-based company. 
Here 's a breakdown of what we should generally be done in a wrongful foreclosure sale, with specific considerations for our situation in Georgia and as a veteran : 1. Act Immediately and Gather All Documents : * We know that Time is Critical : In Georgia, there are strict timelines ( statutes of limitations ) for challenging foreclosures and filing lawsuits. While some claims related to security deeds can have a 21-year statute of limitations if the deed is under seal, other contract-based claims typically have a 6-year period, and you often need to act much sooner to prevent or reverse a sale. 
* Collect Everything : We have gathered every single document related to our mortgage, loan, property, and any communication with all the mentioned entities : * Mortgage statements, payment records, loan modification attempts. 
* All correspondence ( letters, emails ) from lenders, servicers, collection agencies, and legal firms. 
* Foreclosure notices ( Notice of Intent to Accelerate, Notice of Sale, etc. ). 
* Any agreements or contracts that we have signed.We have had 3 Audits that Declared Fraudulent Activity within the Life of the Mortgage * Documents related to the alleged sale of XXXX XXXX shares and the involvement of BofA XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX and XXXX XXXX. This aspect, involving an overseas company and complex corporate structures, is particularly important. 
* Proof of your veteran status. 
2. Consultation with a Foreclosure Defense Attorney in Georgia : * Specialized Expertise is Crucial : This was not a do-it-yourself situation. We have an attorney who specializes in foreclosure defense and real estate litigation in Georgia. They will be able to : * Analyze the Legality of the Foreclosure : Determine if proper procedures were followed, if the foreclosing party had the legal " standing '' ( right ) to foreclose, and if there were any breaches of contract, fraud, or predatory lending practices.

* Investigate the Corporate Connections : The alleged involvement of XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX  BofA XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, and XXXX XXXX, and how shares in one company might relate to your specific property, is a complex legal issue that an experienced attorney can unravel. They can investigate the chain of title and securitization of our loan.

* Identify Grounds for Wrongful Foreclosure : Common grounds include : * Failure to provide proper notice.

* Lack of standing by the foreclosing party.

* Errors in payment accounting.

* Breaches of loan modification agreements.

* Fraudulent practices or misrepresentation.

* Violation of state or federal foreclosure laws.

* Advise on Remedies : If wrongful foreclosure is proven, remedies can include : * Setting aside the sale ( rescission ) : This would reverse the foreclosure and restore our ownership of the property.

* Damages : Monetary compensation for financial losses, emotional distress, and potentially punitive damages if the lender 's actions were egregious.

* Injunctive relief : A court order to reverse the Wrongful foreclosure sale.

* Navigate Georgia 's Specific Laws : Georgia has its own procedures for non-judicial foreclosures ( which happen outside of court ) and judicial foreclosures. Our attorney will know the specific requirements and how to challenge them.

3. Explore Legal Aid and Veteran Resources : * Veteran-Specific Legal Aid : As a veteran, I been told that I have access to specialized legal services.

* VA Resources : The Department of Veterans Affairs ( VA ) and various veteran service organizations ( VSOs ) that can provide referrals to legal aid programs that assist veterans with housing and foreclosure issues. 
* Legal Services Corporation ( LSC ) : LSC funds legal aid organizations across the country that offer assistance to low-income individuals, including veterans.

complaint_what_happened: I have contacted Select Portfolio Service 's Claim Check Center for the past XXXX and a half years requesting them to release our insurance funds that they are holding. SPS is currently holding {$150000.00} and will not release funds so that we can complete rebuilding our home. We speak with a different representative each time and am given a different reason on why the funds are not being released each time I call. Each call takes around XXXX minutes. The reasons for not releasing our funds started with not passing a building inspection that shows how far along we are with the rebuilding. Our first inspection was reported at 20 % by the inspector, and SPS changed it to 10 %. The inspector has stated in her subsequent reports that we were 30 %, 40 % and 60 % completed. These reports were changed by SPS to being only 10 % each time. When we inquired why, we were finally told that they changed what the inspector reported as we didn't have a contract from our builder that provided a final amount. Our contract was for time and materials. So, we provided a contract with the final amount that our builder provided us with, and yet we were still told that we were only at 10 % after the last inspection. We uploaded invoices to the Claim Check Center that we paid to our builder documenting what we had paid of our own money, which exceeds the amount of money that we received from our insurance company. We called the claim check center on XX/XX/XXXX and were told that our issue was being brought to the XXXX level for approval and we would receive a call in XXXX business days. We did not receive a call so we called today and were told that the contract with our builder was not specific enough and they would not pay anything until we provided them with a more detailed contract. The contract from our builder is indeed detailed as it breaks down each cost - flooring, roofing, plumbing, etc. This contract cost us money as it took them a lot of time to write it. The need to provide an even more detailed budget is just a reason for them to delay releasing our funds. We were told on XXXX of our calls, that once we paid 75 % of the cost of rebuilding the home ( based on the bid/contract from the builder ), they would release our insurance money. I have provided paid invoices that document that we have paid more than 75 % of the cost of rebuilding the house. These invoices were reviewed, approved, and accepted by their company. I was told today XX/XX/year> that they would not allow me to speak to the individual/s in charge of making the decisions to deny our request to release our insurance money. We receive a letter every month from SPS that if we do not request and pass an inspection, that they would apply the insurance money to our current mortgage. However, when we asked for them to do this ( at least the money is not just making them more money ), we were told that they would not do this. It is apparent to us that SPS is making a lot of money on the interest our insurance money brings in and we want to file a complaint against them and seek support so that they do the math- add the expenses that we have paid, realize that we have spent more of our own money, and release the final {$150000.00} so that we can finish rebuilding our home and not remain homeless. Being homeless for XXXX years because of the greed of this company is hard to bear.

complaint_what_happened:
I am submitting this complaint regarding Rocket Mortgage / Quicken Loans failure to recognize my lawful status as a Successor in Interest. 

During the relevant period, I was experiencing XXXX-related and medical circumstances and had requested loss-mitigation assistance, including a loan modification. These circumstances affected documentation timing and made reasonable verification alternatives particularly appropriate. Despite notice of these circumstances, Rocket Mortgage denied my Successor-in-Interest request on a technical basis without offering alternatives and continued foreclosure activity instead of engaging in reasonable servicing and loss-mitigation review. 

I occupy the property pursuant to ownership and possessory rights established by a divorce decree. I am not a tenant and have never occupied the property under a lease. 

I submitted a request for recognition as a Successor in Interest. Rocket Mortgage denied my request solely on the basis that my government-issued photo identification was expired. No statute or regulation requiring an unexpired ID for successor-in-interest verification was cited, and no alternative verification methods were offered. 

At the time of denial, Rocket Mortgage had notice of my ownership and possessory rights and my continuous lawful occupancy. Rather than reasonably verifying my identity, Rocket Mortgage imposed a technical barrier and continued foreclosure-related actions. 

Following the denial, foreclosure counsel issued a tenant-based notice under the Protecting Tenants at Foreclosure Act, mischaracterizing my lawful occupancy despite notice that I am not a tenant. 

HUDs Office of Fair Housing and Equal Opportunity acknowledged my complaint but declined jurisdiction, advising that this matter falls under bank regulatory and legal remedies. 

I dispute Rocket Mortgages actions and request regulatory review of its mortgage servicing practices, including failure to recognize successor-in-interest rights and unreasonable verification requirements.

To Whom It May Concern, I am filing a formal complaint against XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX  # XXXX, a licensed mortgage loan originator employed with XXXX XXXX XXXX  ( XXXX, IL ). XXXX XXXX, directly or through his associates at XXXX XXXX, has repeatedly pressured me to execute and deliver a Quitclaim Deed in connection with a pending refinance of our marital property located at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, IL XXXX. 

The issue is not the deed itself, but the timing and handling : I was asked to sign and record the Quitclaim Deed prior to closing and prior to the funding of the refinance loan. 
My attorney responded in writing that this request is unusual, unnecessary, and exposes me to significant risk, since recording the deed before loan funding would remove my ownership interest in the property while I would remain fully liable on the existing mortgage debt. 
Despite this, the request persisted, which I believe constitutes improper and unethical conduct by a licensed mortgage loan originator. 

This conduct, if allowed, would place me in an extremely vulnerable position and directly undermines the fiduciary and professional obligations of a licensed mortgage loan originator to act in compliance with mortgage regulations and consumer protection standards. 

I am requesting that the CFPB investigate this matter to determine whether XXXX XXXX violated any statutes, regulations, or ethical standards by : 1 Requesting the execution/recording of a Quitclaim Deed prior to loan funding ; 2 Placing the consumer ( myself ) at risk of losing ownership rights while remaining obligated on debt ; 3 Failing to follow standard industry practice, which requires that such deeds be held in escrow and recorded only contemporaneously with loan funding. 
Attached are copies of correspondence between my attorney and XXXX XXXX  documenting these requests. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter. Please let me know if you require additional documentation or testimony.

issue: Applying for a mortgage or refinancing an existing mortgage
complaint_what_happened:
Refer to complaint XXXX. In that I wrote the following : We received your complaint. Thank you. 

Earlier in the year Mr Cooper improperly denied my request for a loan assumption ( loan # XXXX ) based on the conclusion that because the property is not my primary residence it does not meet the requirements in XXXX XXXX loan servicing guide for transfers. In fact, XXXX XXXX loan servicing guide DOES have legal language that protects transfers like mine due to divorce and specific language in section 8406.1 sections c and d : " If the transferee requests to assume the Mortgage and/or the transferor requests to be released from liability, then the Servicer must determine the creditworthiness of the transferee in accordance with the requirements in Sections 8406.2 ( a ) and 8406.2 ( b ). '' The word " must '' is a mandate. It requires the servicer to proceed to a credit evaluation, not to issue a summary denial based on occupancy. The servicer is denying based on the opening of the section 8406.1d part ii which mentions primary residence as a condition. The opening sentence is a general condition for when a servicer can't accelerate a loan. However, the final paragraph provides a specific, overriding instruction for a specific action : the request for a release of liability. For reference I was also able to successfully complete the release of liablility process for a second home, a non-primary residence with the loan servicer XXXX Mortgage for loan # XXXX which is also a XXXX XXXX loan. This suggests Mr Cooper 's interpretation of the XXXX XXXX guide is in error. XXXX Mortgage understood a request for release of liability understood the use of the word 'must ' in the regulatory guidelines and provided a process for release of liability. 

Their response to the complaint was a letter in which they stated : On XX/XX/XXXX, we sent an XXXX Welcome XXXX to acknowledge receipt of your inquiry received via telephone on XX/XX/XXXX. Our records indicate we have not yet received the requested documentation. To facilitate a comprehensive review of your request, which was previously denied on XX/XX/XXXX, we require the following essential documents : Required Documentation Assuming Party Questionnaire Selling Party Questionnaire Divorce Decree ( if applicable ) : Please provide documentation showing the property was awarded to the assuming party in the event of a divorce scenario Important Clarification Social Security Number ( SSN ) and Driver 's License ( DL ) are not required if the assuming party is already on the loan. 
We look forward to receiving the necessary documentation to proceed with your request. Please feel free to contact us if you have any questions or concerns regarding the required documents.

To Whom It May Concern : I am writing to file a complaint regarding my mortgage lenders refusal to comply with the provisions of the Homeowners Protection Act ( HPA ) regarding the removal of Private Mortgage Insurance ( PMI ).

According to the HPA, lenders are required by law to automatically cancel PMI when the loan-to-value ( LTV ) ratio reaches 78 % of the original value of the property, provided that the borrower is current on mortgage payments. I have met these conditions, as my mortgage balance has now reached below the 78 % LTV based on the original property value, and I have consistently made my payments on time.

However, my lender has informed me that my loan must be seasoned for two to five years and that I must meet a Loan-To-Current-Value ( LTCV ) of 75 % or less in order to qualify for PMI removal. This requirement conflicts with the automatic cancellation provision under the HPA, which is based on 78 % LTV of the original value, not the current value of the property.

I believe this is a violation of the HPA, as the lender is imposing conditions that are stricter than those mandated by federal law. I am requesting your assistance in resolving this matter and ensuring that my lender follows the legal requirements for automatic PMI cancellation. 

Also there are several Appraisal Concerns : In addition to the non-compliance with HPA provisions, I have significant concerns about the accuracy of the appraisal report used by the lenders chosen appraiser : 1. Comparable Adjustments : The appraisal report states, Due to the scarcity of sales in the area, all comparable adjustments could not be bracketed, and all net adjustments have been positive. This has no negative effect on value nor marketability. While this is noted, the lack of negative adjustments or a more accurate adjustment process may have resulted in an appraisal that does not fully reflect the true market value of my property. 
2. Solar Panels and Backup Batteries : The appraisal report did not consider the solar panels and backup batteries as part of the propertys value, even though these features are financed with an agreement that includes ownership transfer upon full payment. Since ownership will transfer upon completion of the financing agreement, these features should be factored into the homes value as permanent property features. 
3. Property Unit Misclassification : The appraiser did not correctly list my property as having two units. The second unit has its own entrance, a separate bedroom, a full bathroom, a kitchenette, a living area, and a washer and dryer, which clearly qualifies it as a separate living unit. This misclassification may have impacted the final valuation of the property.

Thank you for your attention to this matter. Please feel free to contact me if you require any additional information or documentation.

complaint_what_happened:
SUMMARY OF COMPLAINT We attempted to refinance our mortgage with Freedom Mortgage beginning in early XX/XX/year>. Throughout the process, we received multiple Loan Estimates containing significant and unexplained changes to the loan amount, PMI, interest rate, and cash-to-close. These changes were never proactively communicated to us despite repeated attempts to obtain clarification.

TIMELINE & ISSUES 1. Extreme and Unexplained Disclosure Fluctuations On XX/XX/year>, our Loan Estimate showed + {$8500.00} due back to us at closing. 
On XX/XX/year>, a new estimate stated we owed {$8900.00} at closing. 
This nearly {$17000.00} swing occurred without any change in our financial circumstances. 

The loan amount fluctuated between {$450000.00} and {$450000.00}, and PMI shifted from {$150.00} to {$360.00} with no documentation or explanation. 

XXXX. Incorrect Appraisal Setup & Loss of Original Rate We informed loan officer XXXX that we expected our home to appraise for {$480000.00} {$480000.00}. He instead entered {$500000.00} into their system, creating unrealistic expectations. 

The appraisal that followed was not an inperson inspection. A third-party photographer took photos, and an appraiser reviewed them remotely. The resulting value of {$470000.00} did not account for more than {$34000.00} in verified improvements, including flooring, pool work, electrical updates, and landscaping.

This incorrect valuation setup and appraisal method caused us to lose our originally quoted rate of 5.875 %. 

XXXX. Instruction Not to Make Our Mortgage Payment Freedom Mortgage explicitly instructed us * * not * * to make our XX/XX/XXXX mortgage payment and stated we would have two months without payments during the refinance process.

This turned out to be incorrect. Because of their delays and errors, we were forced to make the payment unexpectedly, causing financial strain. 

XXXX. Unauthorized Rate Increase On XX/XX/XXXX, after speaking with representatives XXXX and XXXX, we were told explicitly that no new disclosure would be issued until further discussion. 

Despite this, later that day we received a Loan Estimate increasing the rate from 6.375 % to 6.675 % without notice, authorization, or explanation. This contradicts TRID disclosure requirements and the assurance we were given. 

XXXX. Forced to Switch Lenders at a Higher Rate Due to Freedoms repeated errors, lack of responsiveness, and disclosure inconsistencies, we were forced to abandon the refinance entirely and move to a new lender. The new loan carries a higher interest rate, increasing our monthly payment and longterm costharm caused directly by Freedom Mortgages mishandling of our refinance. 

FINANCIAL HARM Although Freedom did not collect appraisal or underwriting fees from us, their misrepresentations and failures caused real monetary damage, including : Having to make the XXXX mortgage payment after being told we would not need to. 
Loss of the 5.875 % rate originally quoted. 
Being forced into a higher-rate loan with a new lender.

Increased monthly payment and longterm interest costs.

The increase from 5.875 % to the rate we were forced to accept results in approximately : {$240.00} more per month {$14000.00} in additional cost over 5 years {$86000.00} more in interest over 30 years Please note that we were unable to save all of the disclosures sent to us and Freedom does not offer a way for us to gain access to previously signed documents.
