complaint_what_happened:
To the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau : I am writing to file a formal complaint against Wells Fargo and XXXX XXXX ( dba Shellpoint Mortgage Servicing, successor to Specialized Loan Servicing - SLS ) for failing to inform me of mandatory CARES Act forbearance options, manufacturing fraudulent documentation, engaging in bad-faith servicing, illegally accelerating my loan during a federal foreclosure moratorium, and engaging in the prohibited practice of dual tracking. 

Background My mortgage loan with Wells Fargo is federally backed by XXXX XXXX, making it subject to CARES Act protections and the federal foreclosure moratorium active until XX/XX/XXXX. Due to severe financial hardship from the COVID-19 pandemic, my loan went into default. When the loan was transferred to Specialized Loan Servicing ( SLS ) while in default, they became " debt collectors '' under the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ). XXXX XXXX ( XXXX XXXX Mortgage Servicing ) is the successor to SLS following an acquisition.

Chronology of Events & Timeline Issues Wells Fargo Failures ( Late XXXX XXXX ) : Inaccessible Home Preservation XXXX : My assigned XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX consistently inaccessible and failed to provide guidance on appropriate workout options ( Payment Deferral, Flex Modification, etc. ), which is a violation of servicer duties. 
Failure to Disclose CARES Act Options : Throughout my interactions, I was never informed that the immediate, mandatory CARES Act forbearance was a viable option, despite my documented hardship. 

Unviable Loan Modification Offer & Misinformation : The " Flex Trial Payment '' offer was found much later in general paperwork ; I was never verbally notified about it at the time it was supposedly issued. Wells Fargo proceeded with this offer without proper underwriting or collecting current financial documentation reflecting my pandemic hardship. This offer did not align with XXXX XXXX COVID-19 Flex Modification guidelines designed to ensure sustainable payment reductions. It also doesn't make chronological sense to have a loan modification offer generated before a home preservation specialist ( XXXX XXXX ) was assigned to me to " walk me through the process. '' The offer appears to have been prematurely or improperly generated outside of a proper application process. 

Executive Office Misrepresentation : An executive case specialist, XXXX XXXX, provided explicitly inaccurate information, stating the trial payment was only " designed to bring the account current '' and not necessarily lower my payment. This misrepresentation highlights systemic failure or intentional misinformation. 

Wells Fargo 's Written Admission ( XX/XX/XXXX ) : In their formal response letter, Wells Fargo explicitly admitted : " We are unable to locate documentation from you requesting payment assistance or application. '' This is a critical admission : it confirms my requests for assistance were lost, ignored, or never logged into their system. It invalidates their prior claim that they removed me from assistance for " not responding. '' This proves their process was flawed and likely fabricated. The fact that I was assigned a home preservation specialist ( XXXX XXXX ) specifically to " walk me through the process '' serves as clear evidence that contact was initiated and an application process was, in fact, started. This makes their later claim of no documentation highly suspicious and likely false. 

Improper Property Preservation Fees and Dual Tracking Evidence ( XXXX ) : I was improperly charged property preservation fees starting from the period I was actively engaged with Wells Fargo 's home preservation specialists ( specifically XXXX XXXX and another specialist prior ). My payment history and the detailed breakdowns provided by XXXX confirm these charges. Crucially, the dates of my active engagement with these home preservation specialists and the dates of the property preservation charges clearly corroborate, providing direct evidence that these charges were applied during the time I was actively seeking loss mitigation relief. I have included property service inspection invoices from XXXX XXXX XXXX, working on behalf of Wells Fargo, which are explicitly labeled as " foreclosure inspections '' and dated during this period, as clearly shown in the documents provided. This serves as compelling evidence of illegal dual tracking, a violation of RESPA, as the servicer was pursuing foreclosure activity while my loss mitigation application was pending. 

Specialized Loan Servicing ( SLS ) and XXXX XXXX Servicing Violations ( Post-Transfer and During Moratorium ) : Failure to Provide Debt Validation Notice : Following the loan transfer to XXXX, I was never provided a written validation of debt notice within the required five days, as clearly shown in the documents provided, which is a clear violation of the FDCPA.

Fraudulent Breach Letter and Forgery : Specialized Loan Servicing ( SLS ) generated a fraudulent breach letter associated with an invalid tracking number and an alleged forged signature on the certified mailing receipt. This letter was supposedly sent out during the federal mortgage foreclosure moratorium ( which was active until XX/XX/XXXX ). The servicer 's actions constitute an unlawful acceleration of my loan, as proper written notification was never validly delivered as required by law and the loan agreement, and was prohibited during that timeframe. 
Violation of Foreclosure Moratorium : The issuance of this breach letter and the acceleration occurred during the federal mortgage foreclosure moratorium ( which applied to federally backed loans like mine ), as clearly shown in the documents provided, a clear violation of protections.

Deceptive Reinstatement Practices : I was provided with a reinstatement quote that explicitly stated {$0.00} for legal fees, property preservation fees, late fees, and other fees. However, these fees were, in fact, charged to me, as clearly shown in the documents provided in my payment history. I can also provide a detailed breakdown of property preservation fees that XXXX shared with me each month from XXXX to XXXX. This is a direct contradiction of their written quote and constitutes a deceptive practice under federal law.

complaint_what_happened:
XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX. XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX XXXX XX/XX/XXXX Subject : Request for Reimbursement of XXXX XXXX XXXX Social Security XXXX Account for Mortgage XXXX XXXX XXXX Dear XXXX XXXX XXXX ( Servicing Loan XXXX ), I hope this letter finds you well. I am writing to formally address a matter concerning the payments made by XXXX XXXX XXXX to your company on mortgage loan number XXXX, for the service address XXXX XXXX XXXX. XXXX, XXXX XXXX XXXX  XXXX. It has come to my attention that the funds used to make these mortgage payments were sourced from my Social Security XXXX Account, under the name of " XXXX XXXX. XXXX. '' These payments to your company by XXXX XXXX XXXX are indeed a form of double-dipping on your part, as the alleged mortgage debt was already paid at settlement or within 30 days of you receiving your first mortgage payment. Therefore, the continued collection of payments from my Social Security XXXX Account is both unlawful and unjust, as the debt was already settled at the outset. 

As I understand it, my Social Security XXXX Account is not simply an identification number but a trust account used for the discharge of debt and to fund transactions related to my business dealings. Upon signing the contract with your company and granting you access to my Social Security XXXX, I understood that my mortgage payments and other obligations would be processed accordingly. However, I now believe that your company has continued to collect payments for a debt that was already resolved, and I have concerns regarding the method and legality of these ongoing transactions. 

Additionally, I want to assert my lawful right to rescission under the Truth in Lending Act ( TILA ). According to TILA, 15 U.S. Code 1635, I am entitled to rescind this loan agreement within XXXX years of the transaction date, as your actions appear to have violated key provisions of this statute, particularly those regarding accurate disclosures and the terms of the loan. Furthermore, this violation of XXXX is compounded by your failure to disclose the true nature of the funds being used to pay the mortgage. 

Moreover, your actions also constitute violations of the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act ( RESPA ), particularly concerning the failure to provide proper disclosures related to the servicing of my mortgage. As outlined under RESPA, 12 U.S. Code 2605, you have not adhered to the required guidelines for servicing of loans, including the improper handling of funds. 

In addition, XXXX XXXX protects the integrity of trust accounts from misuse and requires that all actions involving a trust be in accordance with fiduciary duties and lawful purposes. Your company 's actions appear to violate these principles, further substantiating my claim for restitution and the cessation of wrongful transactions. 

Furthermore, in cases such as United States v. Schneider ( 1998 ), the U.S. Supreme Court has affirmed that coercion or unlawful interference with financial transactions or trust accounts is not permissible under U.S. law. Based on these precedents, I assert that payments made to your company from my Social Security Trust Account were done so under duress and without proper consent. 

I am requesting that your company immediately reimburse the funds that were wrongfully collected from my Social Security XXXX Account, as these funds were used to make payments on a debt that was already satisfied. I believe that these payments were made under duress and coercion, and I seek lawful restitution of these amounts. 

Please provide a full accounting of all payments made to your company from my Social Security XXXX Account for mortgage loan # XXXX. I request that you take appropriate measures to resolve this matter promptly and ensure that all necessary actions are taken to reverse the improper transactions. I expect this matter to be resolved within 7 days from the date of this letter. If this issue is not addressed within the given timeframe, I will consider seeking legal advice and pursuing further action to protect my rights, including the exercise of my email at XXXX to discuss this further. 

Sincerely, XXXX XXXX XXXX

complaint_what_happened:
I am filing this complaint regarding Shellpoint Mortgage Servicings improper withholding and misapplication of insurance claim funds related to a covered water loss at my residence located at XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX, TX XXXX. 

The date of loss was XX/XX/XXXX. XXXX XXXX has issued multiple payments related to this claim, including Additional Living Expense ( ALE ) payments, reimbursements for emergency services, and Actual Cash Value ( ACV ) / Recoverable Depreciation payments specifically for repair of the dwelling. 

A Shellpoint processor recently reviewed the claim and advised that the borrower and co-borrower have received {$46000.00} in disbursements, with {$18000.00} identified as prior payments from XXXX XXXX. 

This statement demonstrates the core issue. Shellpoint is grouping together payments that are not construction funds and using those figures to justify limiting the release of insurance proceeds for repairs. 

Payments That Are NOT Construction Funds ALE Additional Living Expenses ( Temporary Housing ) These payments were issued strictly to cover temporary displacement while the home has remained uninhabitable. They are not repair proceeds and can not be used for construction. 

Out-of-Pocket Reimbursements for Emergency and Investigative Services These payments were XXXX for services already completed immediately after the loss and do not represent available repair funds : Water Mitigation XXXX XXXX XXXX : {$6900.00} Leak Detection / Access, Egress, and Regress Services : {$4500.00} These amounts were paid to stop further damage and identify the source of the leak. They were not issued for reconstruction and can not be used toward rebuilding the property. 

State Farm Claim Payments Issued ( Repair-Eligible ACV / RCV Funds ) The following payments were issued by State Farm specifically for repair of the dwelling and are the only funds that should be considered available construction funds : XX/XX/XXXX XXXX Direct Payment : {$3600.00} XX/XX/XXXX XXXX Direct Payment Supplement : {$3600.00} XX/XX/XXXX Cash Value Supplement ( Shellpoint endorsement required ) : {$16000.00} XX/XX/XXXX Supplemental + Recoverable Depreciation ( Shellpoint endorsement required ) : {$16000.00} XX/XX/XXXX ACV Supplement ( Shellpoint endorsement required ) : {$8300.00} These payments total {$47000.00} in repair-eligible insurance proceeds. 

Shellpoints {$40000.00} Limitation and Disbursement Error Despite this, Shellpoint has advised that only {$40000.00} is available for reconstruction. 

From this amount, I have received only {$31000.00} for reconstruction to date. 

This leaves a remaining balance of {$8200.00} that is still owed and should be released prior to any inspection requirement imposed by Shellpoint. 

Shellpoints figures appear to be based on the improper inclusion of ALE payments and emergency service reimbursements in their calculations, which is reducing the amount of funds being made available for actual repairs. 

Harm Being Caused Because Shellpoint is misapplying insurance funds : My home remains uninhabitable Construction is delayed due to lack of fund release My family continues to be displaced I am experiencing financial hardship due to the inability to pay contractors

complaint_what_happened:
I am writing to raise a concern regarding guidance I received from one of Navy Federal 's VA loan agents, XXXX. On XX/XX/year>, she sent me the following email ( copied below ) stating that VA Cash-Out refinances are not available for properties that are owned free and clear and that this is a VA requirement, not only a Navy Federal policy .

Based on official VA guidelines, the VA does permit cash-out refinancing on homes owned free and clear, and there is no requirement that a property have an existing lien. Therefore, I believe the information provided to me may be misleading or inaccurate, and it could impact a veterans ability to access their VA benefits. 

I request that this matter be reviewed and clarified, and that any necessary steps be taken to ensure that accurate VA loan information is provided to members. 

Thank you for your time and attention. 

XXXX XXXX

On XX/XX/year>, RI Housings Assistant Director of Loan Servicing accidentally emailed me another borrowers private mortgage account data payoff figures, loan balance, and other nonpublic details in clear violation of federal privacy laws. This breach had nothing to do with my own account and points to systemic failures in privacy safeguards and staff training. A senior manager admitted the error but offered no evidence of corrective action or regulatory reporting. If this happened once, it may have happened before and without an investigation, it will happen again. 

Unauthorized Disclosure of Private Mortgage Information by Rhode Island Housing Summary of Incident : On XX/XX/year>, XXXX XXXX, Assistant Director of Loan Servicing at Rhode Island Housing ( RI Housing ), sent me an email in which I was inadvertently CCd on a message containing private mortgage account information for another RI Housing borrower. This included personally identifiable loan details, payoff figures, and other account-specific financial data that I had no right to access. 

On XX/XX/year>, XXXX XXXX of RI Housing acknowledged the incident in writing, attributing it to an autofill error. She stated that the affected borrower had been notified, that the incident was documented, and that staff procedures would be reviewed. 

Regulatory Concerns : This incident constitutes an unauthorized disclosure of nonpublic personal information in violation of the GrammLeachBliley Act ( GLBA ) and Regulation P ( 12 CFR Part 1016 ), and potentially other federal and state privacy laws. 

The disclosure was not the result of a technical system failure, but of human error and inadequate controls, indicating a weakness in RI Housings compliance culture. 

This event is separate from my own mortgage account and unrelated to any ongoing dispute I may have with RI Housing it represents a broader compliance and consumer protection failure affecting another borrower. 

This incident may be part of a pattern of misconduct at RI Housing, suggesting systemic deficiencies in privacy safeguards, staff training, and supervisory oversight. 

Requested Action : I request that the CFPB investigate RI Housings privacy controls, incident response, and regulatory reporting procedures, including : Whether this breach was reported to the appropriate regulators. 

Whether RI Housing implemented corrective measures and training to prevent recurrence. 

Whether RI Housing has had similar incidents in recent years, and if so, how those were handled. 

Whether additional supervisory or enforcement action is warranted to ensure compliance with federal privacy requirements. 

Supporting Evidence ( to be attached ) : - Original email from XXXX XXXX with private borrower account data included. 

- Written acknowledgment of breach from XXXX XXXX dated XX/XX/year>. 

- My written reply preserving the incident for litigation and regulatory purposes.

Some clarifying info : -VA XXXX opened in XXXX -Ex-spouse divorced and vacated property in XXXX -Quit Claim Deed signed by ex-spouse ( XXXX ) in XXXX, removing her interest in the property -Ex-spouse remarried in XXXX -Ex-spouse has been desiring to be removed from VA loan for some time now due to applying for her own mortgage ( this is complicating things for her ) -Divorce decree has myself as 100 % liable for mortgage payments and all payments have been made by me since inception of loan in XXXX -Divorce decree from XXXX does have an error where it doesn't clearly indicate property awarding to either party ( the 'Assets ' section has a " XXXX ' next to XXXX XXXX ' for both parties ). This was the reason XXXX signed a Quit Claim Deed in XXXX. 

Communication timeline with lender as follows : XX/XX/XXXX -- sent docs over to Mr. Cooper. Docs included divorce decree and a Quit Claim Deed signed by XXXX in XXXX removing her interest in the property in question. 

XX/XX/XXXX -- Mr. Cooper denied my request citing the need for " a copy of the decree to dissolve the marriage or legal separation agreement verifying the property was awarded to the Veteran ... '' XXXX VA XXXX XXXX. XX/XX/XXXX -- -contacted VA for guidance. After speaking with a VA rep, they stated that the denial reason by the lender isn't an issue with the VA but is a lender interpretation issue that they've heard happens all to often. The rep did recommend that if the lender is denying due to the divorce decree verbiage, that an unrestricted transfer request would also satisfy the intent to remove XXXX from the loan without the need for a divorce decree ( since it is not a VA assumption per their Circular resource ). 

XX/XX/XXXX -- -requested appeal of Mr. Cooper XXXX instead remove XXXX XXXX unrestricted transfer of ownership per VA rep 's guidance. 

XX/XX/XXXX -- Mr. Cooper denied by request citing same reason as before. 

XX/XX/XXXX -- requested another appeal for review with some clarifying info on the divorce decree. I also stated that I didn't feel they were reviewing my case accurately and felt that the lender was displaying UDAAP behavior ( Unfair, Deceptive, Abusive... etc. ) and that I was considering filing a CFPB complaint. Note : This second appeal request did clarify that while the divorce decree did not award either person the property due to an error made in the XXXX filing, that a clear error was made. However, I specified several other items within the document that indicate the intent by myself and XXXX for the property to be awarded to me ( parenting plan indicates myself at this address, mortgage payment liability was awarded to me, furniture was awarded to me, etc. ). This is further validated by having a Quit Claim Deed signed by XXXX in XXXX. 

XX/XX/XXXX -- denied for same reason as before. It is my opinion that Mr. Cooper is not reviewing this case carefully enough and with the completeness of " the whole picture '' in mind. The documents presented when clarified and presented as a complete package, clearly indicate the intent for the property. It is also my opinion that Mr. Cooper 's approach to decision these VA Assumption cases are far too rigid, lack anything other than a hyper-fixated reason to deny the request and thus infringe on the valuable benefits that a VA loan is supposed carry and award to military veterans.

Upon being notified by the XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX in early XXXX  that the tax bill was not paid, I notified SPS via phone that the bill was not paid and that they should pay it immediately. They acknowledged this and we assumed it was taken care of. We were then notified by the township that the bill still wasn't paid and if not paid by XX/XX/XXXX, it would be sent to collections. Once again, SPS was notified and a request was made to pay the taxes with the escrow account. They still did not pay the taxes and the amount due was sent to collections. At this point, the original tax bill of {$19000.00} had increased through fees and penalties to {$23000.00}. complaint_what_happened:
I am currently facing financial hardship and unemployment, which has led me to fall behind on my mortgage payments with Pennymac USA. While I am actively exploring options for relief, the conduct of Pennymac or its affiliated third-party representatives has become unacceptable, invasive, and potentially unlawful. 

On XX/XX/year>, while I was away from home, an individual claiming to represent Pennymac or acting on their behalf arrived at my residence unannounced. This person engaged with my XXXX son, who is a XXXX, and disclosed private financial details, including that I was seriously behind on my mortgage. This disclosure was unauthorized and emotionally distressing for my child. 

In addition to disclosing this information, the individual took photos of the property and behaved in a way that made both of my children feel extremely uncomfortable and intimidated. This is a clear violation of my family 's right to privacy and may constitute harassment under both state and federal consumer protection laws, including the Fair Debt Collection Practices Act ( FDCPA ). 

Furthermore, I have been receiving repeated and aggressive phone calls, which are adding to the mental and emotional stress my household is already experiencing due to these financial difficulties. Rather than offering support or solutions, the approach has been hostile, inappropriate, and damaging to my family. 

I am requesting an immediate investigation into this incident, a formal written response, and that all such actions cease immediately. I also request information on available options for mortgage relief or hardship assistance, as I am committed to resolving the situation responsibly and lawfully.

My husband and I moved to Florida from Missouri in XXXX of XXXX. When looking for mortgage lenders, we found that South State Bank quoted us a significantly less monthly payment for the price range of home that we were looking for. Naturally, we went with South State, due to the decreased expense.

Recently, my monthly mortgage cost increased from {$4100.00} to {$5300.00} without any notice from the company. 

I then made numerous phone calls to obtain an explanation. South State Bank was not the provider to explain despite me calling and talking to at least XXXX departments. I was always told that a file would be submitted and someone would call me back. Although I did receive a message from XXXX, she never called me back after I left at least five messages with her desk. Unfortunately, the person who finally explained what happened was an employee with the property assessor. She explained to me that the homeowners that lived in my home prior to me, had been there a long time and had earned a significant amount of homestead expemptions, which kept their property taxes low. This employee told me that banks are encouraged to base estimated escrow expenses of the appraised value of the home at the time of sale in order to provide more accurate monthly expenses rather than estimating escrow expenses based on the previous owners payments. Because we bough half way through the year, we received the property tax rate that the prior owners paid for the first six months. However, upon the start of XXXX, our property taxes went up. which ultimately prompted the increase in our monthly payment. 
This increase should have been expected by the south state, however, we were never informed or advised that this would happen.

When we bough our house, we were planning on roughly {$4000.00} monthly payments with possible annual fluctuations of a couple hundred dollars per month in the case that insurance or property taxes increased at expected rates. What we were not prepared for was a sudden jump in our annual expenses of over {$13000.00}. 

My husband and I feel that South State did not give us an accurate monthly expense estimate. Although I can not say if intentional, it feels like South State may have based the monthly expense based on the prior owners escrow knowing it would likely create a lower quote.

The rapid {$13000.00} annual increase in expense has placed my family in a difficult situation. We can not pay for our bills and are living above our means due to inaccurate estimates provided to us by South State. If we had been given an accurate monthly quote, we would not have purchased at this price point.

Mr. Cooper is illegally advancing foreclosure while my loan modification application is still under active review. This is a direct violation of both federal servicing laws and Texas foreclosure notice statutes. 

Under federal RESPA regulations ( 12 CFR 1024.41 ), foreclosure activity must stop once a complete loss mitigation application is received. Mr. Cooper sent me a letter on XXXX  confirming my application was complete, yet they continued requesting multiple documents well after that date, and can not give a straight answer on my application status. 

Despite my submitting at least XXXX separate loan modification applications since XX/XX/XXXX, theyve uploaded a foreclosure letter dated XXXX to my account without notifying me while falsely maintaining my application is incomplete during phone calls. 

They are also violating 12 CFR 1024.40 by failing to provide a reliable single point of contact. I was assigned a specialist named XXXX, who has never returned a single call or email. Every time I call, I get a different representative with a different explanation. This obstructs my ability to resolve the issue and is a textbook breach of federal servicing rules. 

Texas Property Code 51.002 requires foreclosure notices to be sent by certified mail and received by the borrower. Mr. Cooper claims they mailed XXXX notices in XXXX, but the USPS tracking number they provided shows the package was never delivered and is still sitting in a USPS warehouse. They never re-sent the notices or verified receipt, but continued to move forward with foreclosure regardless in direct violation of state law. 

They hid the XXXX foreclosure letter in my online portal without sending an email or physical copy. This is the only document out of the many they've sent me over the past 6 months that they failed to alert me to every other correspondence includes an email notification. Also, they are required by Texas law to send foreclosure notices via certified mail. There is no indication this letter was ever physically sent, let alone sent via certified mail. That was intentional and deceptive. 

Mr. Cooper is attempting to fast-track foreclosure by skirting disclosure rules, burying notices, and gaslighting me about my application status. They are breaking the law and they were hoping I wont catch it. Unfortunately for them, I did. 

And I would also like to point out that my home would not even be in foreclosure if it weren't for a Mr. Cooper representative telling me during my first loan modification application to STOP MAKING PAYMENTS until the loan modification is completed. Mr. Cooper has admitted to this false information their employee gave, yet they have done nothing to stop the foreclosure or rectify the situation. They have instead done everything they can to fast track my foreclosure.

Subject : Concerns Regarding Autopay Process and Data Security To Whom It May Concern, I am writing to express my concerns regarding the autopay process for my mortgage, which was acquired by Fifth Third Bank from XXXX XXXX. I do not maintain any other banking relationship with Fifth Third Bank. 

The current procedure for setting up autopay requires me to manually initiate each payment. The alternative option, which is purported to be a true autopay solution, necessitates the completion of a form containing personally identifiable information ( PII ). This form must then be transmitted via mail or fax, both of which are inherently insecure methods of communication. 

Given the sensitive nature of the PII involved, I have serious reservations about the adequacy of privacy protections in this process. I am requesting the ability to establish autopay directly within my online account, thereby ensuring the timely and accurate processing of payments. 

It is concerning that Fifth Third Bank has made this process unnecessarily burdensome, and I urge the institution to facilitate a more secure and user-friendly method for managing mortgage payments. 

Sincerely, XXXX

complaint_what_happened:
I am filing this complaint regarding Shellpoint Mortgage Servicing and their repeated mishandling of a short-sale file for the above-referenced property. This file has been actively worked on since XX/XX/year> and has been plagued by unreasonable counteroffers, inconsistent valuations, and delays that have prevented this estate from completing a legitimate sale. 

The property was originally listed at a price that reflected its true market condition. This home has severe issues, including extensive XXXX mold and major structural concerns, which make it uninhabitable in its current state. Despite the accurate pricing, Shellpoint responded to the first offer with an extremely high counteroffer that made no market sense given the property condition. At Shellpoints direction, the property was relisted at their inflated counteroffer price. 

As expected, the property did not sell at that price and sat stagnant on the market for months. Eventually, a second offer was received at approximately the same price point as the original listingagain accurately reflecting the true value. Shellpoint came back with a small counteroffer slightly above the buyers offer, which the buyer accepted. 

Shortly thereafter, Shellpoint reversed course and issued the same inflated counteroffer from the previous year, which was completely disconnected from the approved negotiation and the propertys condition. It appears Shellpoint simply looked up the outdated listing ( which still showed their prior counteroffer price ) and reused that number instead of reviewing the current valuation or the accepted offer. 

This arbitrary and inconsistent behavior has now jeopardized the sale again. Shellpoints repeated reliance on unrealistic pricingdespite full knowledge of the severe mold and unsafe conditiondemonstrates a failure to review the file in good faith and in compliance with basic servicing standards. 

These actions violate the servicers obligations under RESPA 1024.41, including the duty to : Conduct a reasonable loss-mitigation evaluation Provide consistent and timely review Avoid unnecessary delays and repetitive requests Communicate accurately and in good faith with authorized representatives The estate has complied with every request. The property condition has been fully disclosed. Multiple offers have been submitted. The only barrier to completion is Shellpoints inconsistent and unjustified counteroffers.

I am filing this formal complaint against United Wholesale Mortgage ( UWM ) for multiple, ongoing violations of federal mortgage servicing laws, including ( but not limited to ) violations of the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act ( RESPA ), the Truth in Lending Act ( TILA ), and the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau 's ( CFPB ) Mortgage Servicing Rules under Regulations X and Z..

Nature of the Complaint : 1. Failure to Properly Credit Timely Payments ( 12 CFR 1026.36 ( c ) ( 1 ) ) Beginning in XX/XX/year>, UWM began rejecting timely and valid payments submitted through its online portal and over the phone due to servicing transfer errors.

UWM now claims I am more than 30 days delinquent, even though I have attempted to pay every month on time and in full based on my contractual obligations, and they are holding my XXXX payment in suspense. 

XXXX. Failure to Maintain Accurate Records and Payment Histories ( 12 CFR 1024.38 ( c ) ( 2 ) ) UWM has failed to maintain or provide access to my account history prior to XX/XX/year>. 

My online account was closed and replaced with a new account number, with no access to prior statements, payment records, or transaction histories a clear violation of record retention and borrower access requirements.

UWM agents informed me that servicing records can not be released without a court order, which is false and obstructive.

UWM repeatedly reports inaccurate owed balances online and acknowledges this error.

3. Misapplication of Funds ( 12 CFR 1026.36 ( c ) ( 1 ) ) Overpayments explicitly marked as " principal only '' appear instead to be applied to interest.

This violates both my loan terms and my buydown agreement.

UWM confirmed acceptance of the XXXX payment via phone, but failed to apply the funds to my loan, placing them in suspense without notice or explanation.

4. Incorrect Monthly Billing and Statements ( 12 CFR 1026.41 ) UWMs billing application continues to show inflated or incorrect amounts due ( {$2600.00} and {$2800.00} ) that do not reflect my contractually agreed-upon monthly payment of {$2400.00} under my buydown agreement, utilizing an incorrect percentage rate. 

The online payment system refuses to accept any payment lower than the incorrect amount shown, even when I offer to pay the correct contractual amount. 

As of XXXX, UWM has refused to accept any payment whatsoever, despite continued efforts on my part to pay the amount due under my original agreement.

Agents have confirmed in calls and emails that the original payment amount is correct yet no payment will be accepted unless I pay the inflated amount now shown in the system.

As a result, UWM has falsely reported my account as over 30 days delinquent.

5. Failure to Respond to Error Resolution Requests and Inquiries ( 12 CFR 1024.35 and 1024.36 ) I made repeated attempts ( often daily ) to resolve these issues via phone and online portal.

Calls were frequently disconnected by UWMs automated system, which often rejected my identifying information.

When I did speak with agents, they gave conflicting explanations. Some confirmed error tickets were opened, but not traceable with a number, and no acknowledgments or resolutions were ever issued in writing. Agents frequently promised resolution by a certain date that was then passed and ignored.

UWM has refused to provide internal servicing notes, citing a need for court order, which is both inaccurate and improper.

6. Improper Collections Activity During Active Dispute ( Dual Tracking ) ( 12 CFR 1024.41 ( f ) ) Despite having an active dispute and error review open regarding misapplied payments and a false delinquency, UWM referred my account to its collections department. As this transfer began in XXXX, I am now past the XXXX  day window of protection. 
Collections agents respond to me with questions indicative of foreclosure activity, in violation of dual tracking rules. I am automatically routed to the collections department.

Multiple UWM representatives confirmed that the delinquency was due to UWMs own internal errors, yet collections actions have continued.

7. Inaccessible Customer Service ( 12 CFR 1024.40 ) UWMs phone system repeatedly rejected my Social Security Number and disconnected calls, making communication nearly impossible.

I often waited on hold for extended periods or was forced to abandon calls due to work obligations.

UWMs online chat is mostly automated and does not allow for escalation or effective resolution.

8. Unlawful Withholding of Servicing Records I requested copies of my account history, payment records, and servicing notes.

complaint_what_happened:
I am submitting this complaint regarding a potential violation of the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act ( RESPA ) and Virginia Code 55.1-1006 by CapCenter, a licensed mortgage and title services provider in the XXXXXXXX XXXX Virginia . 

On XX/XX/2025, I called CapCenter and spoke with XXXX ( XXXX ID # XXXX ) regarding a client of mine who had received a Loan Estimate from CapCenter offering XXXX fees for title services under Sections B and C. My client had opted to work with me as their mortgage lender, but was interested in retaining CapCenter 's title services due to the significant cost savings advertised. 

However, XXXX informed me that CapCenter will not allow borrowers to use their title services unless they also use CapCenter as their mortgage lender. I specifically asked whether my client could proceed with CapCenter Title while securing financing through my brokerage, and I was told unequivocally that this was not permitted. 

This practice appears to violate : RESPA Section 9 ( 12 U.S.C. 2608 ), which prohibits conditioning the sale of title services on the use of an affiliated service provider. 
RESPA Section 8 ( a ), which prohibits required use arrangements and unearned fees. 
Virginia Code 55.1-1006, which affirms the buyers right to choose the settlement agent and prohibits requiring the use of a particular one as a condition of the transaction. 
By refusing to honor a consumers independent request to use their title company, CapCenter is engaging in a form of anti-competitive behavior that restricts consumer choice, limits fair competition in the title market, and undermines federal and state consumer protections. 

I respectfully request that this conduct be investigated and that CapCenter be required to revise their practices to ensure compliance with RESPA and Virginia law. I am available to provide additional documentation or witness testimony if needed. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

complaint_what_happened:
I am submitting this updated statement in response to the servicers reply regarding my complaint. 
My concern is not simply that My husband the co-borrower and I the main-Borrower signed documents, but that my husband who is XXXX  XXXX and XXXX did not have a meaningful or accessible opportunity to understand what he was signing. At the time of the loan and throughout the XXXX forbearance and loss mitigation process, the servicer had documentation that my husband XXXX XXXX is XXXX  XXXX and receiving XXXX XXXX benefits. Despite this, no reasonable accommodations were provided to ensure I could review or understand the terms of the Partial Claims. 
The forbearance was presented to us as a temporary pause in payments by XXXX & XXXX throughout the term of the forbearance, and the situation was framed as urgent by Truist Bank, with statements that it was imperative to sign documents to keep the loan in good standing. We were not clearly or effectively informed that the Partial Claims would create liens against the property that would later be repaid from the sale proceeds. Due to my husband 's visual impairment, he was forced to rely on verbal explanations, which I now dispute were clear or sufficient. 
The servicer continues to rely on the fact that documents were signed and notarized. However, a signature alone does not demonstrate informed consent, particularly when a known XXXX affects the ability to review written documents and no accommodations were documented. 
Additionally, the servicer has failed to provide key information relevant to this dispute. I requested call recordings from the XXXX period to verify what was communicated, but I was informed they are no longer available. The servicer has also failed to provide a written call recording retention policy, transcripts, or any detailed documentation beyond general servicing notes to support their claim that my husband and I understood the terms. 
I have also requested all servicing notes, communications, and documentation showing how my husband and my understanding was confirmed and whether any accommodations were considered or offered, but no meaningful evidence has been provided. 
Given these facts, I maintain that the process used to obtain my husband and my agreement to the Partial Claims was not fair, not transparent, and not accessible given my husband 's documented visual impairment. 
I am requesting that the CFPB require the servicer to fully respond to my husband 's and my information requests and review whether proper procedures were followed to ensure informed consent and effective communication under these circumstances. My husband and I also request reconsideration of an appropriate resolution given the lack of accessible communication and missing evidence. 
Thank you for your consideration.

sub_issue: Applying for or obtaining a modification, forbearance plan, short sale, or deed-in-lieu
complaint_what_happened:
I am filing this complaint against Freedom Mortgage Corporation regarding how they are handling my hardship, forbearance, and loss mitigation review on my mortgage. 

I was improperly terminated from my job with XXXX XXXX XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX. Because of this, I have been unable to pay my mortgage since XX/XX/XXXX. I entered into a forbearance plan with Freedom Mortgage due to this hardship. My forbearance plan expired on XX/XX/XXXX. 

Recently, my online account showed that I owed a lump sum payment of {$17000.00} due by XX/XX/XXXX. I contacted Freedom Mortgage because I can not afford to pay that amount due to my ongoing wrongful termination and pending arbitration, and it is impossible for me to make that payment at this time. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I spoke with a representative named XXXX, XXXX  XXXX, over a recorded phone call. During that call, XXXX informed me that I am under review for loss mitigation and for an extension of my forbearance plan. He told me that the system shows the amount of {$17000.00} because the account is behind, but that this amount did not actually apply to me at that time. He said that this amount was only what the system displays, not an amount I had to pay by XX/XX/XXXX, while my loss mitigation review and forbearance extension were being considered. 

Despite this, I later received a message today XX/XX/XXXX stating that I am on a repayment plan. I did not agree to a repayment plan, and I can not afford any repayment plan that requires a large lump sum or significantly increased monthly payments. My arbitration has not occurred yet, and I do not have the income to support that type of plan. Freedom Mortgage is now treating my account as if the repayment plan is valid and as if the {$17000.00} is actually due on XX/XX/XXXX, even after their own representative told me this amount did not apply under loss mitigation review. 

I believe this is misleading and unfair for the following reasons : Freedom Mortgage represented on a recorded call that the listed lump sum amount did not apply to me while I was under loss mitigation and forbearance extension review. 

My account is now being placed on a repayment plan that I did not agree to and can not afford. 
They are effectively creating a situation where I may be pushed toward foreclosure based on a repayment obligation that conflicts with what I was told verbally. 

They sent a generic message claiming that my concerns were addressed, when they clearly were not, because I still can not afford the payment and I never agreed to the repayment plan. 

I am trying in good faith to work with Freedom Mortgage to keep my home and to obtain an appropriate hardship solution while my union grievance and arbitration related to my wrongful termination are pending. I have provided them information about my termination, my union grievance that seeks back pay from the date of termination until reinstatement, and my ongoing hardship.

Additionally, I had previously attempted to make a payment through TD Banks online system and received a timeout error, which prevented the transaction from going through. This further impacted my ability to pay on time. 

I called TD Bank to request a reversal of the late fee, but they denied my request, citing that I had already used a one-time courtesy waiver in 2023. While I respect their policy, I do not believe this fee is fair or appropriate in this case. The delay was clearly caused by the banks restrictive transfer limits and their platforms technical failure. 

I am a responsible customer and I acted in good faith. I believe this fee should be removed as a matter of fairness, and I am requesting the CFPB 's assistance in resolving this matter.

complaint_what_happened:
U.S. Bank artificially projected an escrow account shortage in their analysis and computation based on the unreasonable timing of tax payment which, when paid within XXXX days of the postmark due date, would otherwise project a surplus. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I received an Annual Escrow Account Disclosure Statement from U.S. Bank with the analysis date of XX/XX/XXXX. The trial running balance for the escrow account computation year of XX/XX/XXXX to XX/XX/XXXX, as provided in the statement, reflects a {$320.00} shortage in XX/XX/XXXX. Based on this analysis, U.S. Bank intends to increase our monthly mortgage payment beginning XX/XX/XXXX, to recover the shortage over a XXXX period. 

Under ORS 305.820, property tax payments are considered timely if they bear a postmark date on or before XX/XX/XXXX, regardless of the date they are received by the tax collector. The trial running balance assumes that the property tax payment will be disbursed in XX/XX/XXXX, more than XXXX  days earlier than required. 

If U.S. Bank instead remitted payment after the recurring monthly escrow deposit on XX/XX/XXXX, but still on or before the XX/XX/XXXX tax payment deadline, the escrow account balance would reflect a surplus of {$120.00} instead of a shortage. 

Under 12 CFR 1024.17 ( c ), U.S. Bank is required to ensure the accuracy and reasonableness of its escrow projections. The practice of disbursing payment so far in advance unnecessarily increases the required monthly escrow deposit amount, creating an undue financial burden. If the escrow account analysis reflected the surplus of {$120.00}, U.S. Bank would be required to refund this surplus no later than XX/XX/XXXX per Regulation X, XXXX days after receiving this statement. 

Adjusting the projected tax date from XXXX to XXXX has a significant financial impact over the course of this and future computation years. Consistently projecting tax payments in XXXX, as reflected in both the prior years and this years analyses, has nearly doubled the net present value of shortage deposits compared to projecting the payment in XXXX. This difference is approximately equivalent to XXXX months escrow deposit for taxes and property insurance, creating an undue burden that exceeds the reasonable limits outlined in Regulation X. This methodology also creates a financial incentive misaligned with U.S. Banks fiduciary duty to manage escrow funds in the best interest of borrowers. 

I mailed certified first class a Notice of Error to U.S. Bank at the address provided for such notices in its Annual Escrow Account Disclosure Statement, XXXX XXXX XXXX, XXXX, XXXX  XXXX. XXXX reports this was Delivered to Agent, Picked up at XXXX on XX/XX/XXXX at XXXX ( tracking # XXXX ). 

In speaking with representative XXXX by phone this XX/XX/XXXX, she confirmed a document was received and scanned on XX/XX/XXXX, and that no correspondence has been remitted in response. U.S. Bank has therefore violated 12 CFR 1024.35 ( d ) by failing to provide written response acknowledging receipt of the Notice of Error.

complaint_what_happened:
XX/XX/XXXX : XXXXAmeriHome ( servicer ) sent me a Loan Modification Agreement that was to be signed to close out the Loan Modification process that I had started back in XXXX & been paying on as agreed since XXXX. The letter stated I was to execute the documents in the presence of a notary and return within 5 business days of receipt of this package, but no later than 14 days. 
XX/XX/XXXX : I called Servicer requesting they email me the documentation as I was in XXXX, which I had notified them of 3 weeks prior before I left when they told me the paperwork would be coming. They did email it right away. 
XX/XX/XXXX : after reviewing the documents, I had questions prior to signing. I emailed and received no response by XXXX, so I proceeded with signing hoping that I understood the terms. 
XXXX : I utilized a remote notary to electronically sign the documents as I was still in XXXX. 
XXXX : Documents were printed and sent to XXXX in XXXX via XXXX by my son in XXXX Texas. 
XX/XX/XXXX : Called Servicer after receiving numerous voice messages over the week with not indication as to why they were calling. Servicer stated my account was delinquent and that they hadnt received my signed contract back. I insisted tracking records showed that they had received it. She then confirmed they did receive it before the deadline, it would take up to 30 days to review, and I may receive calls about being delinquent which I should ignore. She also confirmed that all payments have been received as agreed, payments are current, and the next payment due is XX/XX/XXXX. ( XXXX recorded ) XX/XX/XXXX : Called Servicer after again receiving daily calls for a week with voice messages telling me to call, but no reason why. My call was routed to the collections dept after entering my account info. During the one hour call ( recorded ), I was routed thru two other departments ending in the Loss Mitigation. All of them told me that my Loan Modification was rejected because it did not have a wet signature, but none of them could tell me where that requirement was stated in the document including the page dedicated to signing instructions. The last Loss Mitigation person, XXXX, said she needed to get me to a supervisor. After waiting on hold a few minutes she stated the supervisor was busy and would call me back. 
XX/XX/XXXX : 24 hours had passed with no call back from Servicer so I called back. In a complete repeat of the day before I spent over an hour on the call ( recorded ) being passed thru the same channels. Again, no answer as to where it states a wet signature was required until I got to XXXX in the loss mitigation. Important to note that XXXX in Collections stated that I would not receive a callback from Loss Mitigation unless I provided them with application information regarding my current income and expenses. When asked what that had to do with my current situation she did not have an answer other than she has to do it and if I do not give her that information I will not get a return call from the Loss Mitigation Dept. 

Outcome of the last call with XXXX : On the last part of the call she was putting me on hold to discuss with her manager to get an answer to where the wet signature requirement was in the document. Her final stance was that they would not accept the agreement, they could not allow for resigning of the agreement, and I would have to reapply for a new loan modification. She came back with three defences. 

1. She first referred to the Texas Loan Agreement Notice ( pdf page 15 ) that states This written loan agreement represents the final agreement between the parties and may not be contradicted by evidence of prior, contemporaneous, or subsequent oral agreements of the parties. Her first logic was that it says written which implies wet signature ; which I pointed out that would mean that the entire agreement would have to be hand written. 
2. She then went to the signing instructions line ( 12 ) that refers to witness signatures. She started with, this doesnt apply to you, but Nothing in that line indicates a wet signature required, but I pointed out that it was irrelevant since I am not the witness nor was one required in this case so the entire line is irrelevant.

3. She then stated that it is an FHA requirement to have a wet signature. When I asked her what or where that requirement was or if should would send it to me should refused to answer. I then pointed back to her statement in item # 1 which makes any contemporaneous agreement not applicable if not included in this document.

Servicer also said that they did not attempt to remedy the signature issue because they received the document too late to be able to resolve within the original 15 calendar day timeline given. Servicer verified that the document was received on XXXX but was not rejected until XX/XX/XXXX, yet just stated that it was expected that I received, review, notarize, return, they review and return for correction if necessary the documents within 15 calendar days.

Additionally, the payments that I have been making during the trial modification period have not be applied. Somehow Ive gone from being one payment late ( less than {$4000.00} ) to now owing over {$11000.00}.

Transcripts or call recordings available if requested.

I am the owner of a property secured by mortgage loan number XXXX, following the death of the original borrower in late XX/XX/XXXX. As a protected " successor in interest, '' I am not liable for the loan, but I am responsible for ensuring payments are made. My goal has always been to bring the account current and continue payments. 

Following the borrower 's passing, the loan became past due. I made my first attempt to contact Carrington Mortgage Services in XXXX XXXX to inform them of the death and to get instructions on how to proceed. I was told that I would have to wait 30 days for a different department to provide me with a list of the documents required to verify my status and to get instructions on how to pay. I made a second attempt to contact the servicer in XX/XX/XXXX and was given a similar stalling response, with no information provided on how to make a payment to bring the account current. 

In a good-faith attempt to bring the account current, I mailed a check for the full past-due balance in late XXXX XX/XX/XXXX. However, on XX/XX/XXXX, XXXX returned the check via mail to the vacant property address, which I had previously informed them was no longer occupied. They stated they would only accept a certified check for payment. This refusal to provide a reinstatement quote or accept a conforming payment is a violation of federal mortgage servicing rules and directly contributed to the continued delinquency. 

On XX/XX/XXXX, I sent a formal letter to Carrington via both FAX and XXXX Mail to notify them of the death and to submit documents I believed they would need, since they had failed to provide me with a list of required documents. My proactive communication was an attempt to resolve the issue and make a payment. 

Despite my ongoing efforts, the servicer referred the account to foreclosure on XX/XX/XXXX. This was done without providing me with a Notice of Intent to Foreclose, as it was sent to a vacant property address. In XX/XX/XXXX, I ended up researching on my own how to make a payment and successfully brought the loan to a current status on XX/XX/XXXX, by paying the full past-due balance. 

However, my account was subsequently charged over {$1000.00} for " Attorney Fees Billed '' on XX/XX/XXXX. These fees were assessed after the loan was brought current and were a direct result of the servicer 's procedural failures, not a lack of effort on my part. 

In response, I sent a formal Notice of Error ( NOE ) to Carrington on XX/XX/XXXX, disputing the fees under RESPA guidelines and requesting a detailed breakdown of the charges, including the specific legal services performed and invoices from the law firm. 

Carrington 's response, dated XX/XX/XXXX, was wholly inadequate. They acknowledged the loan was reinstated but offered no substantive explanation for the fees, simply stating they were " assessed to the loan in accordance with the terms of the mortgage. '' They failed to provide the detailed breakdown I requested and provided a vague date for when the fees were " incurred, '' stating it was different from when they were " assessed ''. 

Carrington 's conduct represents multiple violations of RESPA : - Failure to promptly facilitate communication with a successor in interest after being notified of the borrower 's death. 

- Failure to accept a conforming payment.

- Imposition of a fee for which they lack a reasonable basis, as the fees are a result of their own procedural failures.

- Failure to conduct a reasonable investigation and provide a substantive response to my Notice of Error, as required by law.

complaint_what_happened:
This is a critical legal issue that we have recently discovered. A foreclosure sale in Georgia without a proper chain of title is likely not legal and may be considered a " wrongful foreclosure. '' The core of a valid foreclosure is that the foreclosing party must have the legal right to do so. Here 's a breakdown of what we found out what that means in Georgia and what happens when that's not the case.

The Role of the Promissory Note and Security Deed In Georgia, when you get a mortgage, you typically sign two key documents : * Promissory Note : This is your promise to repay the loan. It's the debt itself.

* Security Deed : This is the legal document that gives the lender a security interest in our property. It allows them to foreclose if you fail to repay the loan. 
For a foreclosure to be legal, the foreclosing entity must hold both the promissory note and the security deed, or have a legal right to enforce them. When loans are sold, these documents must be properly transferred through a series of legal assignments. This chain of assignments creates the " chain of title. '' The Problem with a " Broken '' Chain of Title If there's a gap in the assignmentsmeaning the loan was transferred but the legal documentation didn't followthe new entity ( in this case, XXXX XXXX XXXX ) therefore the union of VA and XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX may not have had the legal authority to foreclose on our. This is the reason that they can't prove they are the true owner of the debt and the right to the property. 
Why a Foreclosure Sale Without a Proper Chain of Title is Not Legal * Lack of Standing : The foreclosing party must have " standing '' to foreclose. This means they must be able to prove they are the legal owner of the debt and the security deed. Without a proper chain of assignments, they can't establish this. 
* Violation of Georgia Law : Georgia is a non-judicial foreclosure state, meaning the lender doesn't have to go to court to foreclose. However, they must strictly adhere to specific statutory and contractual requirements. One of these requirements is that they must have the legal authority to foreclose. A missing or improper assignment of the mortgage is a fundamental flaw in this process.

* A Voidable Sale : A foreclosure sale conducted without the foreclosing party having the legal right to do so may be considered voidable or even void, depending on the specifics. This means we may be able to have the sale set aside and reclaim our title.

Our Specific Situation We have mentioned that we " have no documents pertaining to the property '' from XXXX XXXX XXXX in our chain of assignments. This is a significant issue and a strong potential defense against the foreclosure. The fact that the property was allegedly " sold as Stock Shares by XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX '' further complicates matters and suggests a potentially fraudulent or irregular transaction. 
What to Do Now : * We will not ignore this.We were informed by our Lawyers Do not move out of the house. 
* We now have a XXXX  XXXX XXXX Litigation and Wrongful Forclosure Attorney. Because it took time to find someone to take our case. This is a complex legal issue that requires the expertise of a lawyer who understands Georgia 's specific foreclosure laws. 
* Our attorney will help us : * File a lawsuit to challenge the foreclosure and request an injunction to stop any eviction proceedings.

* Conduct a thorough title search to trace the complete chain of ownership of the security deed and the promissory note from our original lender to XXXX XXXX XXXX. This will reveal the missing or improper assignments.

* Examine the foreclosure notice to ensure all legal requirements were met.

* We will use the lack of proper chain of title as a primary defense to get the foreclosure sale set aside.

This is a serious legal challenge, and it's essential that we act quickly. Our lawyers can help Us navigate the legal system and protect our rights as the homeowner. 
In God We Trust Psalm 91 AMEN

On XX/XX/XXXX the XXXX representative that I was in communication with told us that townhome pricing would be restored if we were able to provide a document proving that the land is included in the assessed value of the home. That evening I emailed the Maryland XXXX 's property data report that included the land as part of the base value of the property. 

On XX/XX/XXXX ( XXXX  days before close ) XXXX restored our loan pricing based on the XXXX document and we re-locked our rate based on the townhome classification. 

On XX/XX/XXXX ( XXXX days before close ) XXXX called and told us they were reverting to condo pricing based on the title docs that they had just received. sub_issue: Changes in loan terms during or after closing
complaint_what_happened:
I am submitting this complaint regarding a material and unacceptable change to my loan terms four ( 4 ) days before my scheduled closing date of XXXX XXXX. 

The Initial Agreement : My loan agreement was based on the initial Loan Estimate ( LE ) that included an estimated lender charge of {$1300.00}. I also agreed to pay approximately {$1400.00} in discount points to secure a fixed rate of 5.625 %. The total agreed-upon fees were {$2800.00}.

The Disputed Change : On Friday XX/XX/XXXX, my loan officer informed me that a prior waiver or commitment regarding the origination charge had been removed ( LLPA Waiver ). This resulted in an immediate increase of {$5100.00} in lender-charged fees, raising the total fees ( including points ) to {$8000.00}. This change in cost occurred 3 days prior to closing, making me financially captive to the deal.

My loan officer told me that he did not know why the
LLPA waiver was taken away and that he is " fighting it '' and " talking to secondary to get an answer '' but that ultimately is going to be whatever they send in the closure document. I received the closing disclosure at XXXX XXXX  CT on XX/XX/year> meaning after hours ( see attached snip ). 

Regulatory Violation and Unfair Practice : This unexpected and drastic increase in the Origination Charge ( Section A on the LE/CD ) appears to violate the TRID Rule 's fee tolerance requirements, as it was not based on a legitimate " changed circumstance '' and was sprung on me just days before closing.

The timing of this " bait and switch '' tactic constitutes an Unfair, Deceptive, or Abusive Act or Practice ( UDAAP ), as it exploits my lack of time to switch lenders without jeopardizing the purchase contract. Furthermore, this change requires the issuance of a new Closing Disclosure, which automatically forces a delay in my closing.

sub_issue: Trying to communicate with the company to fix an issue while managing or servicing your loan
complaint_what_happened:
I am submitting this formal complaint regarding ongoing mortgageservicing misconduct by LoanCare , LLC ( LoanCare ), acting as the subservicer for XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ( XXXX ). Despite numerous attempts to resolve the matter directly, LoanCare refuses to acknowledge or properly credit a lawful tender of payment, mishandles my account, and continues to threaten foreclosure. 

* * Background * * In XX/XX/2025 I received a monthly mortgage statement from LoanCare instructing me to tender a Partial Prepayment or Partial Payment of principal using the statements registered security payment coupon. The statement explains that such prepayments, when made in accordance with the Note, will be applied to the principal balance unless otherwise specified. 

On XX/XX/2025, I tendered payment of {$36000.00} via registered security instrument, using the payment coupon and following the instructions in the statement, the Note, and HUD form XXXX. My written instructions requested that the payment be applied as a principal reduction to reinstate my mortgage. I mailed this payment via certified mail, and LoanCare accepted it ; however, my account has never been credited. 

Instead of applying the payment, LoanCare responded with boilerplate letters accusing me of socalled sovereign citizen arguments and refusing to honor my tender. They asserted that payments must be in the form of a cashiers check or money order, even though neither the Note nor the mortgage imposes this restriction. LoanCare has kept the payment and continues to assess late fees and interest. 

Over the past two years I have repeatedly requested payoff quotes, account histories, escrow analyses, and proof of ownership of the note. LoanCare either failed to respond or sent duplicate letters that did not address my questions. LoanCares refusal to provide requested documents violates the Real Estate Settlement Procedures Act ( RESPA ) requirements for responding to qualified written requests and notices of error. 

LoanCare continues to report the loan as delinquent and has referred the account to foreclosure counsel. Their refusal to credit my tender of payment has damaged my credit and caused significant emotional distress. 

* * Bases for complaint * * XXXX. * * Misapplication of payments and refusal to credit registered security tender. * * LoanCare accepted my registered security payment but failed to apply it to the mortgage principal. The Note allows principal prepayments at any time without penalty, and the HUD XXXX explicitly states that prepayments may be made without additional interest when tendered on the installment due date. LoanCares refusal to credit the payment violates the Note, HUD regulations, and XXXX XXXX servicing guidance. 

XXXX. * * Failure to respond to Qualified Written Requests ( QWR ) and Notices of Error. * * LoanCares generic response letters did not provide account history, payoff information, or proof of the note holder and therefore do not satisfy RESPA requirements under 12 U.S.C. 2605 ( e ) and Regulation X, 12 C.F.R. 1024.35. 

XXXX. * * Deceptive and discriminatory conduct. * * LoanCare characterized my lawful tender of payment as a XXXX XXXX tactic, which is unfair and defamatory. I am not asserting fringe legal theories ; I simply followed the instructions in the Note and LoanCares own statement. Their refusal to credit the payment and their discriminatory labeling constitute unfair practices. 

XXXX. * * Misrepresentation of payment methods and note ownership. * * LoanCare has not proven that XXXX holds the note or that LoanCare may reject my payment. They insist that only cashiers checks or money orders are acceptable, contrary to the Note which permits payment in U.S. currency or by check. Additionally, my repeated requests for proof of note ownership remain unanswered. 

* * Relief requested * * I respectfully request that the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau : Investigate LoanCares handling of my mortgage and require them to apply my {$36000.00} registered security payment to the principal balance ; Order LoanCare to cease charging interest and late fees resulting from their refusal to credit my payment and to remove any negative credit reporting caused by this issue ; Require LoanCare to provide a full and accurate accounting of the mortgage loan, including payment history, escrow analysis, and identification of the current owner of the note ; Direct LoanCare to reverse foreclosure proceedings and reinstate the loan upon application of my payment ; Take any other action deemed necessary to ensure compliance with federal law and protection of consumers. 

Enclosed with this complaint are copies of my XX/XX/2025 tender, certified mail receipts, relevant pages of the Note and HUD form XXXX, and the responses I received from LoanCare. 

Thank you for your attention to this matter.

complaint_what_happened:
I applied for a home equity line of credit with Figure Lending in late XX/XX/year>. At closing, the notary did not provide me with the required Truth in Lending disclosures or the Notice of Right to Cancel. Figure later admitted in an email that these disclosures were only posted to my online dashboard after signing. 

Within the legal rescission period, I sent Figure a signed cancellation notice. Despite this, Figure funded the loan anyway and deposited {$350000.00} into my bank account. On the same day they funded, they were still asking me to provide proof of hazard insurance, which showed the loan was not even finalized when I canceled. 

When I contacted them, a company representative told me my cancellation was too late and that they could not pull the funds back. This is false under federal law. I also contacted my bank, which opened a fraud dispute case to reverse the ACH transfer.

Consumers should be aware that Figure funded a loan after rescission and refused to honor my cancellation.

sub_issue: Applying for or obtaining a modification, forbearance plan, short sale, or deed-in-lieu
complaint_what_happened:
To whom it may concern, Greetings, I am a veteran  with a VA Home Loan. My mortgage holder is New American Funding. 

I went back to school for XXXX. With the extreme demands of XXXX school, I became behind with some of my payments. At the time I owed around {$8000.00} with fees ECT... I paid {$5000.00}. However my home remained on track with New American Funding for foreclosure. 

I submitted for a loan modification. After submitting all that was required, I was informed that everything was submitted successfully. 

However, I was asked to RESUBMIT. This occurred over, and over and over again. Each time I was informed that they did not complete the modification within the required 30 days, and that it TIMED OUT, so I would need to RESUBMIT AGAIN. 

I learned, that at that time, VA had a HOLD on ALL VETERAN MORTGAGES, VA HOME LOANS. This HALTED ALL ACTIONS or FORECLOSURE 'S for ALL VETERANS FOR XXXX YEAR. 

After A LOT of FRUSTRATION, with REPEATEDLY SUBMITTING for a loan modification, because New American Funding allowed the submission to TIME-OUT..... 
ALMOST, IMMEDIATELY after the year time line of the VA hold on Veterans home loans, they, New American Funding, FINALLY COMPLETED MY MODIFICATION SUBMISSION. 

They replied with a HIGHER INTEREST RATE and PAYMENTS of OVER {$2700.00} DOLLARS!!! 

With additional, frustration, I contacted the VA Home Loans Department. 

The question then became... 
Why was my interest rate raised higher than 1 %, and in addition, why was my loan not refered for the XXXX program. 

This was a program for veterans such as myself, that needed to catch-up their mortgage payments. 

After they realized that they did not refer me as they should have ; they, New American Funding, at that time, informed me that I qualified to request for my loan to be considered for the Veterans XXXX program. 

I SUBMITTED and WAS APPROVED ON XX/XX/XXXX. 
However, NEW AMERICAN FUNDING, ADMITTEDLY, FAILED TO SUBMIT MY APPROVAL WITH THE VA as REQUIRED. 

Their negligence with doing so, resulted in ME, the VETERAN, TO LOSE MY XXXX  VETERANS BENEFIT. 

This, the XXXX benefit, that I was APPROVED TO RECEIVE, would have placed my loan in a status that... 
* LOWERED MY INTEREST RATE TO 2.2 % * LOWERED MY MONTHLY PAYMENT DUE TO {$1900.00} * PLACE THE OVER DUE AMOUNT TO THE REAR OF MY LOAN, ALLOWING ME TO BE BACK ON TRACK WITH MY MORTGAGE. 

BUT INSTEAD ... because of their ADMITTED!!! FAILURE to handle my loan properly with submission to the VA, AS REQUIRED, I LOST MY BENEFITS. 

New American Funding, then sent me another loan modification with a HIGHER than the 2.2 % that I would have gotten on the XXXX program, and a MUCH, HIGHER PAYMENT of over {$2400.00}, rather than the {$1900.00} that I had with XXXX. 

I, UNDER MUCH DURESS, signed this modification, that I thought was unfair and not right, but felt I had no choice with the deadline that was provided. 

I was to resume payments, the HIGHER PAYMENTS on XX/XX/XXXX. 

When I attempted to make a payment, prior to this date, New American Funding BLOCKED me from making a payment. 

New American Funding informed that the documentation was done in ERROR, and that my payments were to start in XXXX INSTEAD OF XXXX and that the PAYMENT AMOUNT WAS WRONG. 

So, New American Funding, then drafted ANOTHER MODIFICATION FOR ME TO SIGN. 

I was then SENT ANOTHER MODIFICATION with an even HIGHER INTEREST RATE of 5.5 % AND PAYMENTS of OVER {$2700.00} DOLLARS!!!
